English: How to manage a brooding hen?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingītungata atia ngūkū ikomereirie?

English: A broody hen should be separated from the flock to prevent other hens from disturbing her.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū īkomereirie ībatīī gūtigithūkanio na ngūkū iria ingī nīguo itikamīthīnie.

English: Keep the hen in a separate nest with free access to fresh water and feed within a short distance
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūmīakīra gītara kīikarītie kīhandū īgūkinyīra maaī na irio ītegūthiī mūno

English: What is brooding?
Kikuyu: Gūkomereria nī gwīka atia?

English: This is the habit of birds sitting on eggs in the nests. It appears in flocks in case of stress or when they are underweight
Kikuyu: Ūyū nī mūtugo wa ngūkū gūikarīra matūmbī gītara-inī. Mūtugo ūyū wonekaga rīrīa ngūkū iratangīka kana ona rīrīa igūithītie ūritū wacio.

English: How to avoid broodiness?
Kikuyu: Hihi tūngīgiria atia ngūkū mūtugo wa kwenda gūkomera?

English: Prevent floor laying.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūgiria ngūkū kūrekeria thī.

English: Close nest in the evening (4 hours to lights out).
Kikuyu: Hinga itara cia kūrekeria hwai-inī, mathaa mana mbere ya ndūma kūingīra.

English: Collect eggs 4-6 times a day.
Kikuyu: Ūngania matūmbī maita mana nginya matandatū o mūthenya.

English: Avoid leaving broody hens to brood on the floor to stop the spread of the behavior.
Kikuyu: Ndūgatige ngūkū ironania kwenda gūkomera ikomererie matūmbī thī nīguo mūtugo ūyū ndūkaiyukio nī ngūkū ingī.

English: Avoid high stocking densities which compromises feeder and drinker space per bird.
Kikuyu: Ndūgakīrie namba ya ngūkū iria ciagīrīire harī kīūmba nīguo ītikahatīkane īkīria na īkīnyua.

English: Make a broody pen. For a 300 sq. m. house the dimension of the pen should be 1sq m. this is enough to accommodate up to 5 birds.
Kikuyu: Thondeka gītara gia gūkomereria. Kīūmba kia ūnene wa 300 sq M kīrabatara gītara kia ūnene wa mita imwe kwa imwe kīrīa kīngīhūthīrwo nīngūkū ithano.

English: How to improve survival rates of chicks (Brooding)
Kikuyu: Tūngīhota atīa kūongerera uhoti wa tūcui gūkūra?

English: Brooding is rearing of chicks until they are able to survive on their own
Kikuyu: Brooding nī kūrera njui nginya rīrīa ikahota kwīikaria.

English: Chicks should be protected from excess heat and cold, starvation, dehydration, predators and rodents
Kikuyu: Njui ibataiī kūgitīrwo kuumana na ūrūgarī mwīngī na heho, kūhūta, kūnyota, ona nyamū ingīcirīa.

English: Which types of brooding exist?
Kikuyu: Hihi njui itūrīkithagio na njīra irīkū?

English: Artificial and natural brooding
Kikuyu: Kwī njīra ya kīīrīu na ya ndūire.

English: What is natural brooding?
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra ya ndūire nī īrīkū?

English: A broody hen rears the hatched chicks (providing heat and help them to get food)
Kikuyu: Ngūkū yarīkia gūkomereria na njui igacarīkio, īcokaga kūrera njui icio na njīra ya gūcigitīra heho na gūciteithia gwetha irio.

English: Put food and clean water near the chicks
Kikuyu: Igīra njui irio na maaī matheru.

English: Protect the chicks from predators and rodents using improvised devices
Kikuyu: Gitīra njui kuumana na nyamū ingīcirīa ūkīhūthīra indo cia gūcingatithia.

English: To reduce deaths, house the hen and chicks in the hut at night, put them in enclosures during the day with quality protein feed
Kikuyu: Nīguo kūnyihia ikuo, njui na nyina ihingirwo ciūmba-inī, ningī ona kūrī mūthenya, ihingīrwo na iheo irio irī na protein njega.

English: What is artificial brooding?
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra ya ūreri wa tūcui ya kīīrīu nī īrīkū?

English: Can be used to rear day-old chicks
Kikuyu: Njīra īno no īrūmīrīrwo kūrera njui irī na ūkūrū wa kuuma mūthenya wa mbere.

English: The following can be used to provide heat: 1. Charcoal jiko: one is adequate for 500 chicks 2. Kerosene lamp: one is adequate for 50 chicks 3. Hay box or Baskets 4. Electricity: one infra-red lamp for 250 chicks or 60-100 watt
Kikuyu: Indo ici no iharīrio nīguo ihe njui ūrūgarī: 1. Njiko ya makara; imwe nī njiganu harī njui magana matano. 2. Tawa wa maguta; ūmwe harī njui mīrongo ītano. 3. Ithandūkū ria nyeki kana mbaketi. 4. Thitima; ngirobu īmwe ya 60-100 watts ya ūtheri mūtune harī njui magana merī ma mīrongo ītano.

English: How to monitor temperature in the chicken house?
Kikuyu: ūngīhota atīa kūramata ūrugarī kīūmba-inī kia ngūkū?

English: Temperature above the floor should be kept at 32oC for the first week, and then lowered by 4oC for each week up to the 4th week
Kikuyu: Ūrūgarī wagīrīirwo kūigwo ūrī gīthimo-inī kia 32oC wiki ya mbere, ūgacoka ūgathiī ūkīnyihagio na gīthimo kia 4oC o wiki nginya wiki ya kana.

English: Observe the reaction of chicks to the heat
Kikuyu: Hebata kūrora kana njui neiratangīka nīūgarūrūku ūyū wa ūrugarī.

English: At correct brooding temperatures, chicks are evenly spread within the brooder ring
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ūrugarī ūrī ithimi-inī iria ciagīrīire, njui ikoragwo ihūrunjūkīte wega thīinī wa haria irarererwo.

English: At low temperature they crowd around the heat source
Kikuyu: No kūngīkorwo kūrī na heho, njui icokanagīrīra ikarigicīria kīhumo kia ūrugarī.

English: At high temperature the chicks will move away from the heat source
Kikuyu: Ūrugarī ūngīkīra, njui cieheragīra kīhumo kiagūo.

English: How to hatch many chicks at once?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingītūrīkia njui nyingī hamwe atīa?

English: Either broody hen or broody duck can be used to achieve this:
Kikuyu: Nīguo ūndū ūyū ūhinge, ngūkū kana mbata irenda gūkomera no ihūthīrwo.

English: Broody hen can be used to sit on up to 15 eggs continuously for at least 2 times by removing hatched chicks every time they hatch and replacing them with new eggs
Kikuyu: Ngūkū no ikomererie matumbī ikūmi na matano irūmanīrīirie maita merī kwa njīra ya kūmiehereria njui na kūmīigīra matūmbī mangī.

English: Ducks can sit on 30-35 eggs and can be used for up to 6 consecutive times
Kikuyu: Mbata no ikomererie matumbī mīrongo ītatu nginya mīrongo ītatū na matano, na noīrūmanīrīrie gūkomereria maita matandatū.

English: Turkey can raise up to 50 chicks
Kikuyu: Mbata mūcinga noīrere njui mīrongo ītano.

English: Hatching many chicks around the same time makes it easy to feed, vaccinate and sell
Kikuyu: Gūcarīkia tūcui tūingī hamwe nīkūhūthagia mīhere ya irio, kūhe njajo ona kwendia.

English: When hens that start laying within the same week get broody, the first hen to become broody can be delayed by being given one (1) egg to sit on.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ngūkū iria ciambīrīirie kūrekia kahinda ga kiumia-inī kīmwe ciambīrīria kūonania kwenda gūkomera, ngūkū īrīa īkuonania īrī ya mbere no īhenererio na njīra ya kūmīigīra itumbī rīmwe.

English: This is repeated for second, third, fourth so that all hens are set on one day
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū ūcokagīrio kūrī ngūkū ya kerī, ya gatatū, ona ya kana, nīguo ngūkū ciothe iharīrio na ciigīrwo matumbī ikomererie ihinda-inī rīmwe.

English: When ready to set, get rid of all the dummy eggs
Kikuyu: Warīkia kūharīria, eheria matumbī mothe mahūthīkīte gūcihenereria.

English: What is the correct size of a laying and broody nests?
Kikuyu: Hihi itara cia kūrekeria na gūkomereria ibataiī gūkorwo na ūnene ūrīkū?

English: A nest box measuring 30 x 30 x 30 cm is enough for 5 layers. Place in a wind free area
Kikuyu: Gītara kīmwe kīrīa kīngīhūthīrwo nī ngūkū ithano gībatiī gūkorwo na ithimo cia sentimita 30 x 30 x 30. Gībatiī kūigwo handū hatakinyaga rūhuho.

English: It is advisable to place the laying nests in the dark part of the house to prevent exposure to the sun
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūiga itara cia kūrekeria handū harīa he nduma thīiniīwa kīūmba kia ngūkū handū riūa rītarakinya

English: A calabash or nest basket may measure 40 x 20 x 25 cm (upper diameter x height x lower diameter). A clay pot is made more or less the same as calabash
Kikuyu: Gītara gia kīihūri kana ona giakītwo na kiondo no gīkorwo na ithimo cia 40cm x 20cm x 25cm. Gītara kia nyungū no kīhanane na gia kīihūri.

English: Any nest material should be removed and burnt after every brood to kill parasites and get rid of diseases especially those made of grass and wood shavings
Kikuyu: Itara ciothe, makīria iria ithondeketwo na nyeki na maranda, no mūhaka ikarutwo na igacinwo o thutha wa ngūkū gūcarīkia tūcui nīguo kūūraga tūgunyū na kūnina mīrimū.

English: Avoid building nests on the ground outside the chicken houses as eggs outside houses are more exposed to predators and thieves
Kikuyu: Ndūgathondeke itara njaa ya ciūmba cia ngūkū nīūndū matumbī no marīo nī nyamū kana ona maiyūo

English: Nests should be placed inside the chicken house and preferably above the ground (1 for every 5 hens)
Kikuyu: Nīwega gwaka itara thīinī wa kīūmba kia ngūkū na makīria handū igūrū (gītara kīmwe harī o ngūkū ithano)

English: Nests should be of the right size for the hen to feel comfortable
Kikuyu: Gītara kiagīrīire gūkorwo gīkīnene ūndū ngūkū īkwīganīra ītarī nathīna.

English: Don’t give food while the hen is in the nest
Kikuyu: Ndūkahe ngūkū irio irī thīinī wa gītara.

English: The first eggs should not be collected or used for hatching
Kikuyu: Matumbī ma mbere matibatie kūrūtwo itara-inī kana ona kūigīrwo hīndī ya gūkomereria.

English: What is a Brooder?
Kikuyu: Hihi bruda nīkīī?

English: A brooder is a special room designed to provide optimal conditions for the growth and development of the day-old chicks in the first three weeks of their lives.
Kikuyu: Bruda nī handū ha mwanya hathondekagwo harongoreirio gūteithia tūcui twa kuuma ūkūrū wa thikū imwe nginya wiki ithatū gūkūra wega.

English: What are the qualities of a good brooder?
Kikuyu: Hihi maūthī ma bruda njega nī marīkū?

English: A good brooder should have the following: 1) Optimum Temperature (start 34o C at the end 28o C) 2) Good Ventilation (oxygen) 3) Optimum light 4) Adequate space (30 to 40 birds per sq. m)
Kikuyu: Bruda njega ībatiī gūkorwo na maūndū maya: 1). Ūrugarīūrīa wagīrīire wakwambīrīria gīthimo kia 34oC nginya 28oC hīndīya kūrīkīrīria, 2).  Rīera rīega (oxygen), 3). Ūtheri wa kūigana, 4). Handū hanene kwūigana (ngūkū mīrongo ītatū nginya mīrongo īna harīo mita īmwe kwa īmwe)

English: What to consider when constructing a brooder?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū wagīrīirūo nī gwīciria ūhoro wamo rīrīa ūraaka bruda?

English: A safe, warm shelter is crucial for raising chicks. A round, wood-shavings filled brooder with a heat source like a jiko ensures their comfort and growth.
Kikuyu: Ūgītīīri na ūrugarīnīmaūndū mabata mīrerere-inīya njui. Bruda yagīrīirwo gwakwo īgīthiūrūrī, īkarwo maranda, na kīhumo kīa ūrugarī gīgekīrwo ta njiko nīguo njui ikūre wega ītegūtangīka.

English: What are perches?
Kikuyu: Hihi ngathīnīkīī?

English: Perches are poles suspended at about 40 cm from the floor for birds to rest on especially at night. They are useful in reducing aggression. The distance between perches should be 40cm and the perch space should be 5 – 10 cm for each bird.
Kikuyu: Ūyū nī mwako wa mītī īcūrītīo ūraihu wa sentimita 40 kuuma thīī thīinī wa kīaga harīa ngūkū ciūmbaga rīrīa ikwenda kūhurūka makīria ūtukū. Mītī īno yagīrīire gūtaganio na warīe wa sentimita 40 na cio ngūkū igatinganīra sentimita 5 nginya 10.

English: What is pecking?
Kikuyu: Gūkanya nī gwīka atīa?

English: When an egg is laid, there is extraversion of the oviduct with high light intensity and poor nest quality; the protruded oviduct can be pecked by other birds causing death.
Kikuyu: Riria ngūkū īrarekia itumbī, njīra ya ūciari nīyumanaga nanja na nīūndū wa ūgwo, kūngīkorwo kūrī na ūtheri mūingī gītara-inī gītarī gīake wega, ngūkū ingī no imīkanyekanye na gūcūngīrīria gīkūo kia ngūkū īyo.

English: How do you prevent pecking?
Kikuyu: Hihi gūkanya kūngīrigīrīrio atia?

English: Use artificial lights with warm colors.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra matawa marī na ūtheri mūthaka.

English: Control light intensity.
Kikuyu: Nyihia kana wongerere ūtheri ūria kwagīrīire.

English: Shade open-sided houses.
Kikuyu: īkīra kīruru ciaga-inī iria itarī ngirimanie mīena.

English: Provide comfortable nests in the right location and numbers.
Kikuyu: Iga itara njega cia kūigana haria ciagīrīire thīinī wa ciaga.

English: Install perches in the production house.
Kikuyu: Thondeka mītī ya kūūmbwo nī ngūkū thīinī wa kiaga kīrīa ūgūcirerera.

English: Avoid feed deficiency and under consumption.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kwaga kūhūtia ngūkū.

English: Provide enough feeder space.
Kikuyu: Nīngī mīharatī ya kūrīra īkorwo arī mīiganu.

English: Ensure a rapid feed distribution system.
Kikuyu: Tigīrīra wīna mūtaratara mwega wa gūita irio mīharaatī-inī naihenya.

English: Avoid crumble and pellet feeds (takes a short time to consume).
Kikuyu: Ndūkahe irio ihana hangarata nīūndū cioyaga ihinda rīnini kūrīka

English: Avoid stress, parasites (internal and external) and enteritis.
Kikuyu: Ehereria ngūkū mītangīko, igūnyū iria irī thīinī wa mwīrī ona igūrū kana ona mīrīmu ta mūthemba ūria ūtūmaga mara maimbe

English: Provide good quality litter to keep birds occupied
Kikuyu: Ita matandīko mega mekūiga ngūkū na mīhang'o ya kūhūria.

English: De-beak properly
Kikuyu: Renga mīkanye wega.

English: What is smothering?
Kikuyu: Smothering nī gūika atīa?

English: It is the crowding together of floor-reared (deep litter) pullets, which may lead to piling, suffocation and death.
Kikuyu: Gūkū nī gūcokanīrīra kwa ngūkū iria ireragīrwo ciaga-inī iria citagwo mahuti na itarī ngayanie, ūndū ūngītūma ithondeke hība, iremwo nī kūhuhia, o na ikue.

English: How to monitor for signs of illness in chicken?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia ngūkū ndwaru imenyekaga atia?

English: Keep an eye out for any unusual behavior or changes in the appearance of your chickens, as these could be signs of illness.
Kikuyu: Ikara ūkīroraga mītugo ītarī ya ndūire kana ūgarūrūkū wa ūrīa ngūkū ihana, nīūndū ici noikorwo arī ndariri cia mūrimū.

English: If you suspect that your chickens are sick, it’s important to isolate them and seek veterinary treatment as soon as possible.
Kikuyu: Angīkorūo nīūkuona ngūkū ciaku irī na mūrimū, nī ūndū wa bata gūcieherania na iria ingī itarī ndwaru na ūcoke ūcarie ūteithio wa ndagītarī wa mahiū naihenya o ūrīa kūngīhoteka.

English: Why do we need to practice good biosecurity measures?
Kikuyu: Hihi gītūmi gīa gūthingatithia ugitīīri wa kīmerera nī kīrīkū?

English: To prevent the spread of diseases, it’s important to quarantine any new birds before introducing them to your existing flock and to avoid mixing birds from different sources
Kikuyu: Nīgetha kūrigīrīria gūtherema kwa mīrimū. He bata kwamba kūiga keheri-inī ngūkū o yothe ng'eni mbere ya kūmīingīria kūrī iria ingī na ningī gwīthema gūtukania ngūkū kuuma ihumo itiganīte.

English: How to handle sick chicken?
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ndwaru ītungatagwo atīa?

English: Sick chicken are usually treated using drugs given in feed or drinking water. This is given to all the chickens in the production unit (both sick and healthy)
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ndwaru īthondekagwo na dawa īkīrītwo irio-inī kana maaī-inī ma kūnyua. Dawa īno īheagwo ngūkū ciothe irī thīinī wa kīaga, ndwaru na itarī ndwaru.

English: How to give medicine to sick chicken?
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ndwaru iheagwo dawa atīa?

English: Mixing drugs with feed should be avoided as sick chickens always lose appetite and are too weak to compete for feed with healthy chickens
Kikuyu: Gūtukania dawa na irio gūtiagīrīire nīūndū ngūkū dwaru nīīremagwo kūrīa wega, na itirī na hinya wa gūcindana na iria itarī ndwaru.

English: Increase in total feed consumption due to other factors increases total intake of all feed ingredients, including drugs
Kikuyu: Gūkorwo ikīrīa irio nyingī nīūndū wa maūndū mangī matiganīte,nīgūteithagia kūhe mwīrī indo cia bata iria ithondekete irio na ningī dawa īrīa yongereirwo.

English: Water medication should be used since sick chickens would continue drinking water even when not eating
Kikuyu: Kūhe dawa īrī maaī-inī nī kwa bata nīūndū ngūkū ona īrī ndwaru no īnyuaga maaī.

English: Medicating all the chickens is useful in putting the disease in check until the chickens develop strong immunity to overcome infections
Kikuyu: Kūhe ngūkū dawa irī ciothe nī kwa bata nīguo kūgirīrīria mūrimū, nginya ihote gwīthondekera ūgitīīri wa kūhota kūrūa na mīrimū.

English: Vaccines should only be given in non-treated (non-chlorinated or distilled) water, as chlorine or other sanitizing agents can destroy vaccines
Kikuyu: Dawa cia njanjo ciagīrīirwo gwīkīrwo maaī-inī matarī na dawa ta chlorine kana kīndū kīngī gīa kūraga njamuthi nīūndū no imīthūkie.

English: Avoid giving vaccine in water or feed for free-ranging chickens as it is not easy for chickens to consume the right dosage. Instead use eye/nostril drops
Kikuyu: Wītheme kūhe njanjo īrī maaī-inī kūrī ngūkū itarī cia kūhingīrwo nīundū nociage kūnyua gīthimi kīrīa kīagīrīire.Na ithenya-inī rīu ūihe na njīra ya gūtatīria ritho kana iniūrū.

English: Increased water intake during hot weather may lead to overconsumption of water medication, while cold weather may result in reduced water intake and thus under dosage of water medication
Kikuyu: Kūnyua maaī maingī hīndī ya ūrugarī no gūcūngīrīrie kūnyūa dawa nyingī gūkīra gīthimi na ningī kūnyua maaī manini nīundū wa heho no gūtūme ngūkū īnyue dawa nini gūkīra gīthimi kīrīa kīagīrīire.

English: If natural sources of water are available the intake by some chicken from the trough may be light
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo ihumo cia kīmerera cia maaī ciīkuo, no igirie ngūkū īnyue maaī ma mūharatī wega.

English: How do healthy chickens appear?
Kikuyu: Hihi ngūkū īrī ūgima mwega wa mwīrī īkoragwo īhana atīa?

English: Externally they are clean, alert and on guard
Kikuyu: īkīoneka nī theru, īikaraga īiguīte na īhūgīte.

English: Have bright eyes
Kikuyu: īkoragwo na maitho matheru.

English: Their comb and wattles are pink
Kikuyu: Kīrera nīkia rangi wa pinki

English: Have raised head and straight neck
Kikuyu: Na mūtwe mūinamūku na ngingo nūngarū

English: Are able to walk, stand, run and scratch
Kikuyu: Nī īhotaga gūthiī, kūrūgama, gūteng'era ona kūhuria.

English: Have strong legs and normal voice (crows)
Kikuyu: Nī irī magūrū marī nahinya na nīikengetaga wega.

English: Have bright eyes and comb
Kikuyu: Maitho na kīrera gīkengu.

English: Continuously eat and drink
Kikuyu: Irīyaga na kūnyua hingo ciothe

English: Soft and compact droppings
Kikuyu: igathiī kioro kiororo na kīnyitu

English: Breathe quietly
Kikuyu: īhuhagia īhoreire

English: Lay eggs normally
Kikuyu: Ikarekia matumbī wega

English: Have smooth neat feathers
Kikuyu: Nīirī maguoya matheru na manyoroku

English: Scales on the feet are smooth and yellow
Kikuyu: Scales cia magūrū nīnyoroku na rangi wa ngoikoni

English: How do unhealthy/sick chickens appear?
Kikuyu: Hihi ngūkū ndwaru ikoragwo ihana atīa?

English: Drop their head and close their eyes
Kikuyu: Ikoragwo ikomereirie mītwe na kūhinga maitho

English: Tired and lifeless
Kikuyu: Ikoragwo arī nogu na itekwīigwa.

English: Dull eyes and comb
Kikuyu: Maitho na irera itarīngengu

English: Sit or lie down
Kikuyu: Ciikaraga ikomete kaingī

English: Eat and drink less
Kikuyu: Irīaga na kūnyua hanini

English: Lay less or stop laying eggs
Kikuyu: Ikarekia matumbī manini kana ona igatiga kūrekia biū.

English: Ruffled and loose feathers
Kikuyu: Maguoya mahurute na maregeru

English: Wet droppings with blood or worms
Kikuyu: Igathiī kīoro kīhūthū kīna thakame kana ona mīnyoo

English: Diarrhoea, cough, sneeze and breathe noisily
Kikuyu: Ikaharwo, gūkorora, kūhaya ona kūhuhia igīcayaga

English: Faeces smear around vent region
Kikuyu: Ikehakania kīoro gatitira-inī

English: Unable to move
Kikuyu: Ikaremwo nī gūthiī

English: Abnormal sounds
Kikuyu: Igakengeta kana kūruta mūgambo ūtarī wa ndūire

English: Legs bent and drooping wings
Kikuyu: Magūrū macio nī magonyoku na nīigūithagia mathagu.

English: What are the common sources of infections in chickens?
Kikuyu: Hihi ihumo nyingī cia mīrimū ya ngūkū nī irīkū?

English: Neighbours: disease outbreaks in neighbouring homesteads can easily spread to your farm
Kikuyu: Mīciī īrīa mūrigainie: Mītuthūko ya mīrimū mīciī-inī īria mūringainie no ithereme kwa ūhūthū na īkinye gwaku mūgūnda.

English: Human: workers, customers and other visitors can bring infections to your farm
Kikuyu: Andū: Aruti a wīra, agūri na ageni no marehere mīrimū gwaku mūgūnda.

English: Recovered carriers: they appear healthy but they continue to shed germs causing diseases in faeces and discharges that can be a risk especially to chicks
Kikuyu: Ngūkū iria ciarīkia kūhona: Imwe ya ngūkū ici, cionekaga irī na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī no no ithiaga na mbere kūruta tūhengereta tūrīa tūrehaga mīrimū kūgerera kioro, ūndū ūrī ūrehaga ūgwati makīria harī tūcui.

English: Housing chickens of different ages together: due to carrier status, varying immunity among chickens of different ages
Kikuyu: Kūhingīra hamwe ngūkū ina ūkūrū ūtiganīte: nīūndū wa gūkua njamuthi, ngūkū irī na ūkūrū ngūrani ikoragwo na ūhoti ngūrani wa gwītiria mīrimū.

English: New introduction: new purchases, gifts etc. may bring diseases to the farm
Kikuyu: Indo ng'eni mūgūnda-inī: Indo njerū, iheo, na indo ingī, no irehe mīrimū mūgūnda-inī.

English: Egg-borne diseases: these are passed from infected hen to new hatched chicks. Some diseases are carried inside the egg shell while others are just on the shell surface e.g. Salmonellosis
Kikuyu: Mīrimū īrehagwo nī matumbī: īno nī mīrimū īrīa ītambagio nī matumbī kuuma kūrī ngūkū iria ndwaru nginya kūrī njui. Mīrimū īno īmwe no īkorwo īrī thīinī wa ītumbī kana igūrū wa ikorogosha ta mūrimū wa Salmonellosis

English: Equipment: sharing of contaminated equipment between farms and failure to clean utensils
Kikuyu: Indo cia wīra: Kūhoyana indo cia wīra ikūīte mīrimū kuuma mūgūnda-inī ūmwe nginya ūria ūngī na kwaga gūthambia wega indo cia kūrīra na kūrūgīra.

English: Others: Rodents, household pets, wild birds, insects, feed
Kikuyu: Indo ingī ni hamwe na mbīa, nyamū iria ireragīrwo nyūmba irī ciagūkenia andū, nyoni cia gīthaka, na irio cia mahiū.

English: What are the symptoms of Newcastle Disease?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia mūrimū wa Newcastle nīirīkū?

English: It is caused by a highly contagious virus that infects the respiratory and nervous system.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūrehagwo nī njamuthi mūthemba wa virus īgwatanagio mūno īria īhutagia mahūri.

English: In both chicks and old birds’ mortality varies from zero to 100 percent depending on the virulence of the organism.
Kikuyu: Ikuo cia njui na ngūkū no ikoragwo na ūtiganū mūnene, ūū atī no ikue ciothe kana ciathe gūkua onea īmwe, kūringana na ūrūrū wa njamuthi īyo.

English: The disease is spread to chicken by direct contact with droppings or discharges from infected birds.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūtambagio na njīra ya ngūkū kūhutania na kioro kana gīko kīngī kia kuuma mwīrī wa ngūkū kana nyoni iria ndwaru.

English: It affects all poultry including chicken, ducks, turkeys, pigeons but chicken are the most affected.
Kikuyu: Ūnyitaga nyoni cia mīthemba yothe hamwe na ngūkū, bata, bata mūcinga, ona ndutura, no ngūkū nīcio irwaraga kaīngī.

English: Symptoms include: Egg production declining up to zero in four days.
Kikuyu: Ndariri nī hamwe na ngūkū kūnyihia matumbī marīa irarekia na thutha wa thikū inya igakorwo itigīte kūrekia.

English: When laying resumes, misshapen eggs are produced with rough and sometimes bleached shells.
Kikuyu: Hīndī ya kūrekia yacokerera, matumbī maumaga na mūmbīre ūtarīwa ndūire, makorogoca matarī manyoroku na maumu rangi.

English: In chicks, gasping coughing and sneezing is observed, nasal discharge, depression, and diarrhea.
Kikuyu: Njui nīiremagwo nīkūhuhia, igakorora na gwathimūra, ikaruta kīmira, ikahorera na ikaharwo.

English: Birds may be seen sitting on their back hock joints; others may walk backward, or in circles; or hide their head between their legs.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū iria ndwaru ituragia maru, nacio ingī igīthīa na kīhutatī, kana gīthiūrūrī, kana ona ikahitha mūtwe gatagatī ka magūrū.

English: In adult birds, external symptoms are more of respiratory nature and sudden decrease in egg production.
Kikuyu: Ndariri ironeka cia mūrimū ūyū kūrī ngūkū īrarekia nī iria ikonainie na mahūri na kūremwo nī kūhuhia na kūnyihia kana ona gūtiga kūrekia o rīmwe.

English: Birds lose appetite and are droopy.
Kikuyu: Nyoni nīciagaga thuti wa kūrīa na ikenda gūikara cinamīrīire.

English: If the infection is severe, most birds sit on the floor and the roosts.
Kikuyu: Ūrūrū wa mūrimū ūngīneneha, nyoni nyingī ikomaga thī na itara-inī.

English: In severe illness, other symptoms include swelling of the head, twisted neck, and leg paralysis
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ūrūrū wa mūrimū wakīra,ndariri ingī cionekaga nī hamwe na kūiba kwa mūtwe, ngingo thiore na gūkua kwa magūrū

English: What are the symptoms Gumboro/infectious bursal disease?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia mūrimū wa Gumboro ūria ūikaine ta infectious bursal disease nī irīkū?

English: It is a highly contagious viral disease that affects poultry but mainly chickens particularly at three to eight weeks old.
Kikuyu: Ūyū nī mūrimū ūgwatanagio naihenya mūno ūrīa ūrehagwo nī njamuthi mūthemba wa virus na ūnyitaga ngūkū na kaingī njui cia wiki ithatū nginya wiki inyanya.

English: The chicken gets infection directly on contact with infected birds or indirectly through contact with material contaminated with infected fecal matter.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū inyitagwo nī mūrimū ūyū īmwe kwa īmwe na njīra ya kūhutia iria ndwaru, kana ona kūhutia indo ta mai ma ngūkū iria ndwaru.

English: Contaminated clothes, beddings, equipment, and other materials can spread the disease to a flock.
Kikuyu: Indo ingī ikuīte mūrimū ūyū ta nguo, matandīko, indo cia wīra, ona indo ingī no iteithīe gūtheremia mūrimū ūyū.

English: The virus causing this disease is stable in the environment and particularly difficult to eradicate once it infects a flock.
Kikuyu: Njamuthi mūthemba wa virus īrīa īrehaga mūrimū ūyū nī irī hinya kweheria ciarīkia kūnyita rūūru rwa ngūkū.

English: Symptoms include severe prostration, watery whitish diarrhea, soiled vent feathers, inflammation of the cloaca and eventually death.
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia mūrimū ūyū nī ngūkū kūinamīrīra mūno, kūharwo coka, maguoya mahake mai gatitira-inī, kūiba na thutha kana cloaca, na mūthia gūkua.

English: What are the symptoms Fowl Typhoid?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia mūrimū wa Fowl Tyhoid nī irīkū?

English: The disease is caused by a microorganism called salmonella gallinarum or shigella gallinarum.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūrehagwo nī njamuthi cīatagwo salmonella gallinarum or shigella gallinarum.

English: Symptoms include dullness, ruffled feathers, paleness of the head drooping comb, loss of appetite and pale orange coloured diarrhea.
Kikuyu: Ndariri nī hamwe na kwaga gūcanjamūka, maguoya mabararūku, mūtwe mūhūnyūku, kīrera kīinamu, kwaga thuti wa kūrīa, na kūharwo kioro kia rangi mūtheuku wa orange

English: Symptoms appear in three to four days after infection and death occur in two weeks.
Kikuyu: Ndariri ici cionekaga thikū ithatū nginya inya thutha wa ngūkū kūrwara, igacoka gūkua thutha wa wiki igīrī.

English: The disease can be introduced into the flock by infected birds, materials, shoes, litter, etc
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū no ūtambio nī ngūkū ingī iria ndwaru, indo, iratū, matandīko hamwe na indo ingī.

English: What are the symptoms Fowl Cholera?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia mūrimū wa fowl cholera nī irīkū?

English: The disease is caused by pasteurella avicida, a microorganism that multiplies very rapidly in the blood causing poisoning.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūrehagwo nī njamuthi mūthemba wa Pasteurella avicida, īrīa īciaranaga naihenya mūno thīinī wa thakame na kūmīroga

English: Sick birds, wild birds, human, animals or utensils transmit the disease.
Kikuyu: Nyoni ndwaru, nyoni cia gīthaka-inī, andū, nyamū kana ona indo cia kūrīra na kūrugīra nī itambagia mūrimū ūyū.

English: Symptoms include a yellowish colouration on birds’ droppings, which is followed by yellowish or greenish diarrhea.
Kikuyu: Ndariri nī hamwe na ngūkū gūthiī kīoro kīna rangi mūtheūku wa ngoikoni, kīria gīcoka kūrūmīrīrwo nī rūharwo rwa rangi wa ngoikoni kana ngirini

English: Infected birds become droopy, feverish and sleepy.
Kikuyu: Nyoni iria ndwaru cikaraga cinamīte, ina ūrugarī mūingī na kwenda gūkoma

English: The birds also sit with the head down or turned backward or rested in feathers around the wing.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ici ningī ikomaga igūithītie mūtwe kana ikaūroria nathutha kana ikaūhitha ithagu-inī.

English: The disease spreads very rapidly in a flock.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū  ūtheremaga naihenya mūno

English: What are the symptoms of Pullorum Disease?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia mūrimū wa Pullorum nī irīkū?

English: Pullorum disease is caused by a microorganism called Salmonella Pullorum, which infects the ovary of the hen
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Pulorum ūrehagwo nī njamuthi mūthemba wa Salmonella Pullorum, īria īrwaragia kīga kia matumbī ma ngūkū.

English: The microorganism can also be found in the intestine of chicks.
Kikuyu: Njamuthi ici nīcionekaga thīinī wa mara ma njui.

English: The disease is usually spread by eggs laid by birds carrying the microorganism and chicks hatched from those eggs.
Kikuyu: Kaingī, mūrimū ūyū ūtambagio nī matumbī marīa mareketio nī nyoni iria ndwaru kana njui iria icarīkagio kuuma matumbī-inī mareketio nī nyoni icio ndwaru.

English: Symptoms: Chicks utter squeaky chirps and appear drowsy and ruffle.
Kikuyu: Ndariri nīhamwe na tūcui kūnyingira, tūkoneka twīna gītoro na maguoya mambararūku.

English: The vent is sometimes smeared with fecal discharges.
Kikuyu: Rīmwe nathutha gatitira-inī hakoragwo hahakanītio mai.

English: In adult birds, no external symptoms are seen.
Kikuyu: Nacio nyoni ng'ima itionanagi ndariri o ciothe.

English: In chicks, symptoms are seen in four to 10 days with death occurring in three weeks.
Kikuyu: Tūcui twonanagia ndariri thutha wa thikū inya nginya ikūmi kuuma twa nyitwo nī mūrimū ūyū na tūgakua thutha wa wiki ithatū.

English: What are the symptoms of Fowl Pox?
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia fowl pox ya ngūkū nī irīkū?

English: Fowlpox is a highly infectious skin disease.
Kikuyu: mūrimū wa Fowl pox nī mūrimū wa ngothi na ūtheremaga naihenya.

English: It is characterized by typical pox lesions in the form of wart-like scabs on the face, comb, and wattles.
Kikuyu: wambagīrīria na thundo na tūronda tūrīa tūcokaga kūhana batata ūthiū-inī na kirera-inī.

English: It causes painful sores on the featherless skin and/or the respiratory and digestive tract.
Kikuyu: ūtūmaga igīe na ironda irī na ruo kūria gūtarī maguoya kana mūmero wa irio na wa rīera.

English: Chicken are infected through contact with scabs from infected birds or through blood-sucking insects such as mosquitoes.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū irwaraga mūrimū ūyū kumana na kūhutania na irema cia ironda cia ngūkū īrīa ndwaru, kana kūgerera tūtambi tūrīa tūnyuaga thakame ta rwagī.

English: Symptoms include: Blisters which enlarge into painful yellow bumps and finally to dark coloured wart-looking scabs in the skin in non-feathered areas such as comb, wattle, face, and eyelids.
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia mūrimū ūyū nī ta: Kūgīa na iroruha iria icokaga kūneneha tarī ihūha na mūico ikaira ta mbatata ngothi-inī kūrīa gūtarī maguoya ta kireera-inī, ūthiū na kīmone-inī.

English: Difficulty in breathing and eating due to diseases affecting the respiratory system, mouth, and throat.
Kikuyu: Gūkorwo na ihinda iritū rīrīa īkūhuhia kana īkūrīa nīūndū mūrimū ūyū nīūhotomagia njīra ya kūhuhia, kanua na mūmero.

English: What are the symptoms of Coccidiosis?
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia mūrimū wa kīhuruto nī irīkū?

English: The disease is caused by coccidian, which multiplies very rapidly in the intestines.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa kīhuruto ūrehagwo nī njamuthi īrīa īingīraga thīinī wa mara na ikaingīha na njīra nene.

English: It usually occurs at eight to 10 weeks of age and normally expresses itself in acute and chronic forms.
Kikuyu: Mūno makīria ūnyitaga ngūkū irī na ūkūrū wa ciumia igīrī nginya ciumia ikūmi, na ngūkū īka hinyīrīrīka na njīra nene.

English: In the acute type, death occurs in five to seven days.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa mūrimū wa kīhuruto woka na ūritū mūnene, ngūkū īkuaga thīinī wa thikū ithano nginya mūgwanja.

English: The chronic type does not kill immediately but persists for long.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū mūthemba ūngī ndūūragaga hauhau no nī wūikaraga gwa kahinda Karaya.

English: Symptoms: Infected birds or chicks become droopy, look unthrifty, and usually have ruffled feathers, pale beaks, and shanks.
Kikuyu: Ndariri: Ngūkū kana njui īrīa ndwaru irekagia mathagu, ikarīa na wūitangi, maguoya makaambūrūrūka na mūkanye na mīthirimo īrī na rangi mūthitu.

English: Caecal coccidiosis has bloody droppings.
Kikuyu: Kīhuruto kīa mūhuko wa irio gītūmaga ngūkū īthiī kīoro kīna thakame.

English: Mortality may be high and sudden.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū no ikue irī nyingī o rīmwe.

English: How do you treat Coccidiosis?
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa kūharwo ūrigitagwo atīa?

English: Treatment of Coccidiosis: Good thing, Coccidiosis if noticed early, it can be treated easily.
Kikuyu: Kūrigita mūrimū wa kihuruto: ūndū mwega nī ati, mūrimū ūyū ūngimenyeka tene no ūrigitike raithi.

English: To contain the Coccidiosis outbreak, treat each and every chicken in the flock.
Kikuyu: Nīguo mūrimū wa kīhuruto ndūgathereme, wagīrīire nīkūrigita ngūkū ciothe cirīrūūru-inīrūu

English: Amprolium is the most common treatment of Coccidiosis. It weakens the parasite’s ability to multiply.
Kikuyu: Dawa ya amprolium nīyo īhūthīkaga kaingī kūrigita mūrimū wa kīhuruto. īkoragwo na ūhoti wa kūnina hinya wa njamuthi ikaremwo gūciarana.

English: How is Amprolium administered to the chicken?
Kikuyu: Dawa ya amprolium īheagwo ngūkū na njīra īrīkū?

English: Treatment is administered to the poultry birds by adding Amprolium to their drinking.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū īrigitagwo mūrimū wa kīhuruto na njīra ya kuongerera dawa ya amprolium maaī-inī.

English: Occasionally, medication is administered orally in instances where the birds are not eating or drinking enough water.
Kikuyu: Rīmwe, dawa īno no īkundio na kanua rīrīa ngūkū ītarahota kūnyua maaī kana kūria irio njiganu.

English: Treatment of Newcastle disease?
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa kīhuruto ūrigitagwo atia?

English: There is no specific treatment for Newcastle disease although, to prevent secondary infection, Isolate all the infected poultry birds from the healthy ones. Afterwards, contact your vet immediately.
Kikuyu: Gūtirī ūrigiti wa mwanya wa mūrimū wa newcastle no nīgetha ūrigīrīrie kūrwara rīngī, no weheranie ngūkū iria ndwaru na iria  itarī ndwaru ūcoke wīte ndagītarī wacio naihenya.

English: To prevent Newcastle from occurring, vaccinate your birds after three months.
Kikuyu: Nīguo kūrigīrīria mūrimū wa kīhuruto kūhanīka, nīwega kūhe ngūkū ciaku njanjo o thutha wa mīeri ītatū.

English: Treatment and management of fowl pox?
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa fowl pox ūrigitagwo atīa?

English: Prevent further spread by isolating the sick birds, clean and disinfect drinkers, feeders and chicken house daily.
Kikuyu: Wagīrīire kūrigīrīria gūtherema na njīra ya kweherania ngūkū iria ndwaru, gūthambia na dawa ya kūūraga njamuthi indo cia kūnyūīra, kūrīīra na nyūmba o mūthenya.

English: Unfortunately, there is no cure for fowl pox.
Kikuyu: ūndū wa kīeha nīatī, mūrimū wa fowl pox ndūrī kīhonia.

English: Comfort therapy and preventive measures for secondary infections can be given to affected birds.
Kikuyu: ūrigiti wa kūhoreria na kūrigīrīria kūrwara rīngī nī ciabata kūrī ngūkū iria hutie.

English: Glucose, tetracycline and multivitamins can be administered in drinking water.
Kikuyu: Glucose, dawa ya kūūraga njamuthi mūthemba wa tetracycline o na multivitamins no cīikīrwo maaī-inī ma kūnyua.

English: To treat scabs, dilute iodine solution and ointment every day until they get healed.
Kikuyu: Njīra ya kweheria ngūcī ni kūhaka mūtukanio wa iodine na maguta o mūthenya nginya rīrīa kīronda gīkahona.

English: Prevention and treatment of Infectious Coryza?
Kikuyu: Kurigīrīria na kurigita mūrimū wa kugwatania wa coryza?

English: Prevention is the only method to prevent the disease from occurring.
Kikuyu: Kūgitīra nīkuo njīra njega ya kurigīrīria mūrimū kuoneka.

English: The best way to avoid Infectious Coryza is the Isolation method.
Kikuyu: Njīra njega ya gwithema na mūrimū wa coryza ni kweherania iria ndwaru na iria itarī ndwaru.

English: Keep your chickens protected from other random chickens.
Kikuyu: Gitīra ngūkū ciaku kuumana na gūtukana na ingī ng'eni.

English: Keep their coop and water clean to prevent the disease.
Kikuyu: Iga kiaga giacio kīri gītheru na maaī marīa iranyua marī matheru nīguo kūrigīrīria ndwari.

English: How do chicken birds get the infection from Infectious Coryza?
Kikuyu: Ngūku irwaraga mūrimū wa kugwatanio wa coryza na njīra irikū?

English: The disease is spread to chicken by direct contact with droppings or discharges from infected birds.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa coryza ūtheremagio ni ngūku kūhutania na kioro kana giko kingī kiria kiumaga mwīri kia nyoni īri na mūrimū.

English: Objects such as shoes, equipment, clothing and other materials contaminated with infected feces can spread the disease to new flock.
Kikuyu: Indo ta iratu, indo cia wira, nguo kana indo ingī iria ihutanītie giko kīa mwīrī kīa nyoni ndwaru noitheremie mūrimū rūūru-inī rwerū.

English: What are the signs in infected birds when they have Infectious Coryza?
Kikuyu: Nī ndariri īrīku cionekaga harī ngūkū iria ndwaru mūrimū wa kūgwatanio wa coryza??

English: Symptoms in chicken may vary from mild to severe disease.
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia mūrimū ūyū noikorwo itarī na morūrū kana o na ikorwo irī na morūrū maingī.

English: Disease severity depends on the type of virus and immunity of the birds.
Kikuyu: Ūrūrū wa mūrimū ūringanaga na mūthemba wa njamuthi na uhoti wa ngūku wa kurua na mīrimū.

English: Affected chicken may show signs of coughing, sneezing, nasal discharge, depression and diarrhoea.
Kikuyu: ngūkū iria hutie ni mūrimū uyu ikoragwo na ndariri ta kuhaya, gwathimura, kuruta kimira, kuhorera na kuharwo.

English: In severe illness, other symptoms include swelling of the head, twisted neck and leg paralysis.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ūrūrū wa mūrimū wakīra,ndariri ingīcionekaga nīhamwe na kūiba kwa mūtwe, ngingo thiore na gūkua kwa magūrū

English: In very severe cases, sudden death of birds occurs.
Kikuyu: Ūrūrū ūngīkira, gīkuo kīa hi na hi hari ngūkū gīkoneka.

English: How do I protect my chicken from Infectious Coryza.?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingī gītīra ngūkū ciakwa atia kuumana na mūrimū wa kūgwatanio wa coryza?

English: The virus stays in the environment for a prolonged period. It is important to prevent birds from infection.
Kikuyu: Njamuthi mūthemba wa virus iria irehaga mūrimū ūyū no itūrī kahinda karaya riera-inī. Nīwega kūgitīra ngūkū kūnyitwo nī mūrimū ūyū.

English: This can be achieved through cleaning and disinfecting chicken houses and equipment.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū no ūkinyanīre na njīra ya gūthambia kīaga na indo iria ihūthīkaga ūkīhūthīra maaī mongereirwo dawa.

English: For commercial farms, there is need to restrict entry of people into the chicken house.
Kikuyu: Mīgūnda ya biacara ībatiī kūnyihia andū arīa maraingīra thīinī wa kīaga.

English: Use of footbaths is also important.
Kikuyu: Thondeka handū harīkīragwo dawa nīguo andū makarangithia magūrū.

English: Newly introduced birds should be isolated for several weeks before mixing into the flock.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ng'eni no mūhaka ikaigwo keheri-inī wiki cigana ūna mbere ya itūkanīte na ingī.

English: Since chicken are most affected, there is need to reduce contact between chicken and other poultry such as ducks, turkeys, pigeons and others.
Kikuyu: Na tondū ngūkū nīcio ihotagwo mūno nī mūrimū wa kūgwatanio wa coryza, niwega kwamūrania ngūkū na nyoni ingī ta mbata, mbata mūcinga, na ndūtūra.

English: This disease has no cure but fairly affordable vaccines are available in many agrovets.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ndūhonagwo, no nī ūrī njanjo ītarīgoro ndūka-inī nyingī cia agrovets

English: The vaccines are commonly administered through the eye, nostril or in drinking water.
Kikuyu: Njanjo icio ihūthīkaga na njīra ya gūtatīīria ritho, iniūro, kana ona gūtūkania na maaī ma kūnyua.

English: Vaccination is done three times a year. The vaccine can be given to chicken of all ages.
Kikuyu: Njanjo īno ithecagwo maita matatū o mwaka.

English: It is important to vaccinate only healthy chicken.
Kikuyu: Hebata ngūkū iria irī na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī ikorwo nīcio ikūheo njanjo īno.

English: What to do when birds are infected?
Kikuyu: Ingīka atīa ngūkū ciarwara?

English: Isolate all the sick birds. Contact your vet immediately
Kikuyu: Amūrania ngūkū ciothe ndwaru. Menyithia ndagītarī wa mahiū.

English: Do Not Slaughter and eat the sick birds or Sell the sick birds
Kikuyu: Ndūgathīje kana kūrīa na kwendia ngūkū ndwarū.

English: How do birds get infection?
Kikuyu: Hihi ngūkū inyitagwo nī mūrimū atīa?

English: Chicken get infection directly on contact with infected birds or indirectly through contact with material contaminated with infected feces.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū inyitagwo nī mūrimū īmwe kwa īmwe kūgerera kūhutania na ngūkū iria ndwaru, kana ona kūhutania na indo iria ihutanītie na kīoro kia ngūkū kīna mūrimū.

English: Contaminated clothes, beddings, equipment and other materials can spread the disease to a flock.
Kikuyu: Indo ingī ikuīte mūrimū ūyū ta nguo, matandīko, indo cia wīra, ona indo ingī no iteithīe gūtheremia mūrimū ūyū.

English: The virus causing this disease is stable in the environment and particularly difficult to eradicate once it infects a flock.
Kikuyu: Njamuthi mūthemba wa virus īrīa īrehaga mūrimū ūyū nī irī hinya kweheria ciarīkia kūnyita rūūru rwa ngūkū.

English: How do I protect my chickens from the gumboro disease?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingī gitīra atīa ngūkū kūnyitwo nī mūrimū wa gumboro?

English: Prevention of spread of the virus into your flock is vital.
Kikuyu: Kūgirīrīria njamuthi mūthemba wa virus iria īrehaga mūrimū ūyū kūingīra ngūkū-inī nīūndū wa bata mūno.

English: This can be achieved by restricting movement of people into the farm, footbath and wheel baths on farm entrance.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū no ūhoteke na njīra ya kūgiria andū gūchera mūgūnda-inī, gūīkīra dawa kīhingo-inī handū ha andū kana ngari kūrangīthia magūrū ikīingīra mūgūnda.

English: Isolation of new birds for observation before mixing with the flock.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ng'eni ciambe cigwo keheri-inī ciīrorerwo kana noikorwo na mūrimū mbere ya gūcitukania na iria ingī.

English: Vaccines are available in most agrovets for protection of chickens against Gumboro.
Kikuyu: Njanjo cia mūrimū wa Gumboro nīcionekaga nduka-inī nyingī cia agrovets.

English: These are administered via the eye or drinking water. Injectable vaccines are also available.
Kikuyu: Njanjo ici itatagīrio maitho-inī kana ikaheyanīrio na maaī. Ningī ngūkū no ithecuo.

English: For commercial farms, it is important to buy chicks from farms that vaccinate their breeding stock against Gumboro so as to protect chicks during the first 10-14 days post hatching.
Kikuyu: Migūnda ya biacara ībatiī kūgūra tūcui kuuma mīgūnda īrīa īheaga ngūkū cia gūciarithania njanjo ya Gumboro nīguo kūgitīra tūcui thikū cia mbere ikūmi nginya ikūmi na inya thutha wa gūcarikio.

English: It is important as a farmer to vaccinate your chicks following the vaccination schedule provided by the breeding farm.
Kikuyu: Hebata mūrīmi kūrūmīrīra mūtaratara wa kūhe njui njanjo ūrīa ekūneo nī mūgūnda wa gūciarithania ngūkū.

English: Most breeding farms recommend vaccination of chicks at 14 days and a booster at 24 days of age.
Kikuyu: Mīgūnda mīingī ya gūciarithania ngūkū yendekithagia njui iheo njanjo ina ūkūrū wa thikū ikūmi na inya na īcokerithio thikū ya mīrongo īrī na īna.

English: What to do when your birds are infected with gumboro disease?
Kikuyu: Hihi batiī gūīka atīa ngūkū ciakwa cianyitwo nī mūrimū wa Gūmboro?

English: There is no cure for the disease but support therapy such as multivitamins can be given to affected birds.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ndūri kīhonia no njui iria ndwaru no iheo multivitamins arī ūrigiti wa kūhoreria.

English: Isolate sick birds. Contact your vet immediately
Kikuyu: Amūrania ngūkū iria ndwaru. Menyithia ndagītarīwa mahiū.

English: How to protect your chickens from the FOWL POX disease?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingīgitīra atīa ngūkū kūnyitwo nī mūrimū wa Fowl Pox?

English: Restrict movement of people into and out of the farm, disinfection of equipment, watering and feeding troughs and quarantine new birds brought to the flock.
Kikuyu: Nyihia mīthiīre ya andū kūingīra na kuma mūgūnda, thambia indo cia wīra na mīharatī ya kūnyuīra na kūrīra na maaī mongereirwo dawa nīguo kūūraga njamuthi, na ningī ngūkū ng'eni ikaigwo keheri-inī mbere ya ītukanītio naingī.

English: Control of mosquitoes and other blood sucking arthropods is also important.
Kikuyu: Girīrīria rwagī na mīthemba ingī ya tūng'aurū na tūnyamū tūngī tūrīa tūnyuaga thakame.

English: Vaccines are available for use in day-old chicks and unaffected adult birds using the wing-stab method.
Kikuyu: Njanjo nīcionekaga na ibatiī kūhuthīka harī tūcui twa ūkūru wa mūthenya ūmwe na ngūkū iria itarī ndwaru kwa njīra ya gūtheca ithagu-inī.

English: What to do when your birds are infected by fowl pox disease?
Kikuyu: Ūngīka atia ngūkū ciaku ingīnyitwo nī mūrimū wa fowl pox?

English: When your flock is infected, the first step is to prevent further spread.
Kikuyu: Undū wa mbere nī kūgīrīrīria mūrimū gūtambia makīria warikia kūnyita ngūkū.

English: Isolate sick birds, clean and disinfect waterers, feeders and chicken house daily.
Kikuyu: Amūrania ngūkū iria ndwaru, thambia mīharatī ya maaī na irio na nyūmba yothe ya ngūkū ūkīhūthīra maaī mongereirwo dawa.

English: There is no cure for the disease but comfort therapy and preventive measures for secondary infections can be given to affected birds.
Kikuyu: ūrigiti wa kūhoreria na kūgīrīrīria kūrwara rīngī nīkwabata harī ngūkū iria hūtie.

English: Glucose, tetracycline and multivitamins can be administered in drinking water.
Kikuyu: Glucose, dawa ya kūūraga njamuthi mūthemba wa tetracycline ona multivitamins no cīikīrwo maaī-inī ma kūnyua.

English: Scabs can be treated with dilute iodine solution and ointment (sulfur powder and Vaseline) daily until the lesions heal.
Kikuyu: Njīra ya kweheria ngūcī ni kūhaka mūtukanio wa iodine na maguta (mūtūkanio wa sulfur poweder na vaseline) o mūthenya nginya rīrīa kīronda gīkahona.

English: How to boost Immunity in chicken?
Kikuyu: Ingīogerera atīa hinya wa mwīrī wa ngūkū wa gūitiria mīrimū?

English: Improving chicken health by using feed supplements and additives that work to optimize the consumption of nutrients.
Kikuyu: Kūgacīrithia ūgima mwega wa ngūkū na njīra ya kuongerera indo irīna mūrio irio-inī

English: Reducing the microbial load of feed and raw materials, improving digestive function, and restoring healthy populations of gut bacteria can all be accomplished with high-quality additives.
Kikuyu: Kūnyihia njamuthi iria irī irio-inī ona indo-inī cia gūthondeka irio, kwagīrithia mūthīīre wa irio, na gūteithia gūcokereria tūgūnyū tūrīa tūrī ūguni mwīrī-inī na njīra ya kūongerera indo irī na ūguni mūnene irio-inī.

English: What are the common Kienyeji chicken diseases, their symptoms, and treatment options?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīrimū īrīa kaingī īnyitaga ngūkū cia gīcejī nī īrīkū, ndariri nī irīkū cia mīrimū īyo na īngīrigitwo atīa?

English: Newcastle Disease: Symptoms include respiratory distress, sneezing, coughing, and watery eyes. This is a viral disease, and there is no cure, but a vaccine is available to prevent it.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Newcastle : Ndariri ikoragwo ta kūremwo nī kūhuhia, gwathimūra, kūhaya, na kūrīra maitho. Ūyū nī mūrimū ūrehagwo nī virus na kuoguo ndūrī kīhonia, no nī kūrī njanjo ya kūgirīrīria.

English: Fowl Pox: Symptoms include raised scabby lesions on the face, comb, and wattles. It’s a viral disease, and there is no cure, but vaccination can prevent it.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Fowl pox : Ndariri ikoragwo ta kūgīa ngoni igūrū wa ironda ūthiū na kīreera-inī. mūrimū ūyū umanaga na virus na kuogwo ndūrī kīhonia no nī kūrī njanjo nī giragīrīria.

English: Coccidiosis: Symptoms include diarrhea, loss of appetite, and decreased activity. It’s caused by protozoan parasites and can be treated with anti-coccidial drugs.
Kikuyu: coccidiosis / kīhuruto: Ndariri nī ta: kūharwo, kūremwo nīkūrīa na gwīkuna. Umanaga a njamuthi cia protozoa na no urigitwo na dawa cia kīhuruto kana anti-coccidial.

English: Avian Influenza: Symptoms include respiratory distress, fever, and sudden death. This is a viral disease, and there is no cure, but a vaccine is available to prevent it.
Kikuyu: Avian influenza kana homa ya ngūkū : Ndariri nī ta kūremwo nī kūhihia, ūrugarī mūingīna gīkuū kia hi na hi. Mūrimū ūyū umanaga na virus na ndūrī kīhonia no nī kūrī njanjo ya kūgirīrīria.

English: Marek’s Disease: Symptoms include paralysis, loss of appetite, and weight loss. It’s a viral disease, and there is no cure, but vaccination can prevent it.
Kikuyu: Marek's disease kana kuonja: Ndariri nī ta kuonja, kūremwo nī kūrīa na gūte kīro, mūrimū ūyū umanaga na virus na kuoguo ndūrī kīhonia no njanjo nī giragīrīria.

English: Gumboro Disease: Symptoms include diarrhea, loss of appetite, and depression. It’s caused by a virus and can be prevented by vaccination.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Gumboro: Ndariri nī ta kūharwo, kūremwo nī kūrīa na kūhorera. Mūrimū ūyū umanaga na virus na kuogwo na ūgirī rīirio na njanjo.

English: Fowl Typhoid: Symptoms include high fever, diarrhea, and sudden death. It’s caused by bacteria and can be treated with antibiotics.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Fowl typhoid: Ndariri nī ta ūrugarī kūingīha, kūharwo na gīkuū kia hi na hi. Mūrimū ūyū ūrehagwo nī njamuthi cia bacteria na no urigitwo na dawa cia kūūraga njamuthi.

English: Infectious Bronchitis: Symptoms include respiratory distress, nasal discharge, and watery eyes. It’s caused by a virus and can be prevented by vaccination.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa infectious bronchitis: Ndariri nī hamwe na kūremwo kūhuhia, kūruta kīmira na kūrīra maitho. Mūrimū ūyū umanaga na virus na kuogwo no ūgirīrīirio nī njanjo.

English: What is the treatment of Newcastle disease?
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Newcastle ūrigitagwo atīa?

English: Newcastle disease has no cure; chickens already showing symptoms/signs should not be vaccinated.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Newcastle ndūrī kīhonia. Ngūkū irīa  irīkītie kuonania ndariri ciaguo itiagīrīire nī kūheo njanjo.

English: How do you prevent Newcastle disease?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingīhota atīa kūgirīrīria mīrimū wa Newcastle?

English: The only effective means of preventing Newcastle disease is by protecting chickens from getting the disease through vaccination.
Kikuyu: Njīra īrīa ya mūmwe yūīkaine ya kūgirīrīria mūrimū wa Newcastle no kūgitīra ngūkū kuumana na kūrwara mūrimū na njīra ya njanjo.

English: How to collect, store and select eggs for hatching?
Kikuyu: Njīra njega ya kūngania, kūiga na gūthuura  matumbī ma gūcarīkia?

English: Remove the eggs once laid and stored them in a cool place
Kikuyu: Ūngania matumbī marekio na umaige handū hatarī na ūrugarī mūingī

English: When collecting eggs, do not frighten the chicken as a chicken being disturbed constantly may abandon the nest and not brood the remaining eggs
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ūkūngania matumbī, ndūkamakie ngūkū nīūndū cingīmakio kaingī noitiganīrie matumbī marīa ikomereirie.

English: How to store eggs?
Kikuyu: Matumbī maigagwo atīa?

English: Eggs should be stored at between 15C to 18C depending on the duration of storage.
Kikuyu: Matumbī magīrīire kūigwo na ūrugarī wa gatagatīKa 15°c na 18°c kūringana na kahinda karīa matanyītwo kūigwo.

English: Keep the eggs with the narrow end of the shell down for short term storage and for long term (more than 7 days), with the broader end down.
Kikuyu: Iga matumbī na mwena ūrīa mūceke wa ikorogoca ūrorete nathī marīa matanyītwo kūigwo gwa kahinda kanini na marīa matanyītwo kūigwo gwa kahinda karaya kana makīria ma thikū mūgwanja, mwena ūcio mwari īūkorwo nathī.

English: Pack eggs on wooden pallets.
Kikuyu: Banga matumbī tray-inī ūcoke ūmaige mbareti-inī cia mbaū.

English: Do chicken require proper nutrition?
Kikuyu: Hihi ngūkū nī ibataraga irio njega?

English: Kienyeji chickens are omnivorous and will eat a variety of foods, including grains, insects, and greens.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū cia gīcenjī irīaga irio cia mīthemba yothe hamwe na mūthemba wa mbembe, igunyū na mahuti.

English: It’s important to provide a balanced diet to ensure the chickens are healthy and produce high-quality eggs.
Kikuyu: Nīwega wa kūhe ngūkū irio ina ūtonga wa indio ciothe iria ibataranagia nīguo ikorwo na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī na irekie matumbī mega.

English: Feeding young chicken?
Kikuyu: Hihi ngūkū iheyagwo irio atia?

English: Young chickens should be fed separately from the adults.
Kikuyu: Njui ibatiī kūheo irio kando na ngūkū ng'ima.

English: They should have a special diet and it is best for them not to have to compete with the adults for food
Kikuyu: Ciagīrīirwo kūheo irio cia mwanya na nīwega njui ici itigatharane irio na ngūkū iria ng'ima.

English: Feeding laying hens
Kikuyu: Hihi ngūkū irarekia ibatiī kūheo irio atia?

English: Laying hens should have easy access to calcium rich food that may be supplemented by calcium-rich sources such as crushed snail or egg shells (may eat eggs due to Ca deficiency)
Kikuyu: Ngūkū irarekia ibatiī kūheo irio irī na calcium nyingī īrīa īngīoneka na njīra ya kuongerera indo ta makorogoca morage ma ndinoho kana ma ngūkū(ngūkū no irīe matumbī ingīaga kūigania calcium).

English: How to store feeds for chicken?
Kikuyu: Hihi irio cia ngūkū cibatiī kūigwo atīa?

English: Feed consumption vary with weather (hot vs cold) changes; breed, type, strain, and age of bird; body weight; rate of lay; energy and fiber content of feed; and particle size of feed ingredients
Kikuyu: Mūrīre wa irio ūcenjagia kūringana na rīera (ihiū kana ihehu) ūrīa rīracenjia, mūhīrīga wa ngūkū, mūthemba wa ngūkū, na ūkūrū wa ngūkū, ūritū wayo, ūria īrarekia, mūigana wa irio cia hinya na fiber irio-inī, ona ūrīa irio icio ithīītwo.

English: Store feed in a clean and dry area.
Kikuyu: Iga irio handū hatherū na hatarī na woigū.

English: For free-range, best scavenging time is early morning and late in the evening when insects are plenty and heat less
Kikuyu: Ithaa rīega ria ngūkū kwīyethera irio nī kīroko tene na hwa-inī rīrīa igūnyū irī kwa wūingī na ūrugarī nī mūnini.

English: What is Starter diet or (Chick Mash)?
Kikuyu: Hihi irio cia kwambīrīria njui kana irio cia njui cia mūtu, chick mash, nī irīkū?

English: High in protein (18%, 11.8 Mj ME); offered from day old up to 8 weeks; Each chick will consume about 2 kg during this period.
Kikuyu: Ici nī irio irī na wūingī wa protein ithimo-inī cia 18% na 11.6 Mj ME, iria iheagwo kuuma mūthī wa mbere gacui gacarīkio nginya wiki ya kanana. Njui irīaga mūigana ta wa kilo igīrī ihinda-inī rīrī.

English: Chick mash are given from day 1 to day 56 (8th week), 60g of feed per day @50/= per kg of feed
Kikuyu: Irio cia njui cia mūtutu iheagwo kuuma mūthī wa mbere nginya thikū 56(wiki ya kanana), grams mīrongo ītandatū o mūthenya iria irī na ngarama ya shilingi mīrongo ītano harī kilo.

English: What is Growers’ diet/mash?
Kikuyu: Hihi irio cia kūhehe njui hūre nī irīkū?

English: Lower in protein (14%, 11.6 Mj ME); offered from 9 weeks up to 18 weeks;
Kikuyu: Ici nī irio itarī na wūingī wa protein(14%, 11.6 Mj ME), iria iheagwo kuuma wiki ya kenda nginya wiki ya ikūmi na kana.

English: Each grower will consume about 8 kg during this period.
Kikuyu: Njui imwe hūre īrīaga kilo ta inyanya ihinda-inī rīrī.

English: Growers’ mash are given from day 57 (9th week) to day 133 (19th week), fed 90g of feed per day @35/= per kg of feed
Kikuyu: Irio cia mūtu cia njui hūre iheagwo kuuma thikū ya mīrongo ītano na mūgwanja(wiki ya kenda) nginya thikū ya igana rīmwe ria mīrongo ītatū na ithatū(wiki ya ikūmi na kenda), ikaheo grams mīrongo kenda o mūthenya ya irio ciīna ngarama ya shilingi mīrongo ītatū na ītano harīo kilo.

English: What is Layers diet/mash?
Kikuyu: Hihi irio cia ngūkū irarekia nī irīkū?

English: Medium in protein (14%, 11.2 Mj ME); offered to hens from 19 to 75 weeks.
Kikuyu: Ici nīirio itarīna protein nyingī(14%, 11.2Mj ME); iheyagwo ngūkū kuuma wiki ya ikūmi na kenda nginya wiki ya mīrongo mūgwanja na ītano.

English: Allow 120 g of feed per bird per day. Hens consume about 45 kg of feed annually.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ciītīkīrio kūrīa grams igana rīa mīrongo īrīo mūthenya. Ngūkū irarekia no irīe kiro mīrongo īna na ītano o mwaka.

English: Which farming systems or methods of poultry exist in Kenya
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra cia kūrīithia ngūkū būrūri-inī wa Kenya nī irīkū?

English: Best chicken farming systems in Kenya include deep litter systems, slatted floor system and battery cage systems under the intensive production method.
Kikuyu: Njīra njega cia kūrīithia ngūkū būrūri-inīwa Kenya nīhamwe na gūcirīithīria cīaga gīite mahuti, cīaga cia thirīti na cīaga irīna tūnyūmba twa kūhingīra ngūkū arīnjīra ya ūbacīrīri mūnene wa kūrīithia ngūkū.

English: What Chicken Production Systems in Kenya exist?
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra cia kūrīithia ngūkū būrūri-inīwa Kenya nīirīkū?

English: Chickens are reared under three (3) production systems in the country: free-range, semi-intensive and intensive production systems.
Kikuyu: Kwī njīra ithatū cia kūrera ngūkū būrūri-inī; Njīra ya ngūkū gwītuīra, mūtukanio wa ngūkū gwītuīra na kūhingīrwo hanini, na ngūkū kūhingīrwo biū.

English: What is Free range production system (for indigenous chickens)?
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra ya ngūkū cia gīcenji gwītuīra nī īrīku?

English: This is carried out mainly in rural areas mostly for household consumption and occasionally income generation
Kikuyu: īno nī njīra ya kūrīithia ngūkū cia kūrīa na rīmwe kwendia īrīa kaingī īrūmangīrīrwo icagi-inī.

English: Farmers keep small flocks ranging from (1-50), commonly 5-15 indigenous chickens
Kikuyu: Arīmi marīithagia ngūkū nini(1-50) kaingī ithano nginya ikūmi na ithano.

English: The chickens are housed only in the evening either in the main house (enclosed baskets) with the household members, kitchen or small-structured chicken houses
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ihingagīrwo o hwainītu ikunīkanīirio na gīterenge nyūmba-inī nene kūrīa andū maraikara, kana riko, kana kiaga gia cio.

English: Are allowed to scavenge outside for food during the day (legumes, grains, worms, insects among others)
Kikuyu: Ngūkū nī ciītīkagīrio kwīmathīra irio mūthenya ta mīthemba ya mboco, mbembe, igūnyū, tūtambi na ingī nyingī.

English: Feeding is occasionally supplemented with locally available materials such as remnants of ugali, maize grain etc
Kikuyu: Rīmwe na rīmwe ngūkū nī cikagīrio irio ingīiria iria ironeka o kūu ta matigari ma ngīma na mbembe, arīcia kuongerera kīrīa irīire mūthenya-inī īkīmathīra.

English: Little input is invested towards health services and hens incubate the eggs until they hatch
Kikuyu: Nī mbeca nini ihūthīkaga kūringita ngūkū na nīngī ngūkū nīcio ikomagīrīria matumbī.

English: High death rates arising from diseases such as Newcastle and predators
Kikuyu: Ikuo nyingī nī cionekaga kuumana na mīrimū ta Newcastle na nyamū iria irīaga ngūkū.

English: Long broody periods *Chickens are less productive (about 60 eggs/hen/year)
Kikuyu: ihinda iraya ria kwenda gūkomera; Kwoguo ngūkū ikarekia matūmbi manini (ta matumbī mīrongo ītandatū harī ngūkū imwe o mwaka)

English: Mainly use family labour and are cared for mostly by women and children
Kikuyu: Kaingī, bamīrī na makīria atumia na ciana, nīo matungataga ngūkū.

English: Hens starts laying eggs at 24-30 weeks (6-7.5 months) of age
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ciambagīrīria kūrekia irī na ūkūrū wa kuuma wiki mīrongo irī na īnana nginya mīrongo ītatū(mīeri ītandatū nginya mūgwanja na nuthu).

English: Most chickens produce 2-4 clutches of eggs a year (10-12 eggs per clutch)
Kikuyu: Ngūkū nyingī irekagia matumbi maita merī nginya mana o mwaka na o rita, ikarekia matumbī ikumī nginya ikumī na merī.

English: What is Semi-intensive chicken production system (indigenous and improved kienyeji)?
Kikuyu: Hihi mūtaratara wa kūrīithia ngūkū cia gicenji na njīra ya gūtukania ngūkū gwītuīra na kūhingīra hanini nīūrīkū?

English: In this system, free ranging is supplemented with additional feeding, vaccinations, good hygiene and sanitation
Kikuyu: Thīinī wa mataratara ūyū, nīūndū wa gwītuīra, ngūkū nī iheagwo irio cia kuongerera, njanjo, na ūtheru

English: Require relatively high capital investment compared to extensive production system
Kikuyu: Mūtaratara ūyū ūbataraga mbeca nyingī kūwambīrīria kūrī wa kūrekereria ngūkū

English: Flock sizes range mostly from 50 to more than 200
Kikuyu: Ngūkū nī cīgīkagaa nyingī kuuma mīrongo ītano nginya magana merī.

English: Chickens have a small house where feeders, drinkers and laying nests are put, and where the chickens are housed at night
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ciakagīrwo kīaga kīnini gīa kūkoma ūtūkū na nokwo mīharatī ya irio, maaī na itara ciīkīragwo

English: There is a fenced area outside the chicken house where the chickens exercise, feed, drink, protected from predators and closely observed during the day
Kikuyu: Kīaga gīkī nīgīcokaga gīkarīgīcīrio na rūgirī rwa kūhīta ngūkū rīrīa ikwenda gūcera, kūria, kūnyua ona nīngī gūcigitīra kuumana na kūrī nyamū ingīcirīa, na gūteithia gūcitungata mūthenya-inī.

English: The chicken house should be cleaned regularly and kept dry at all times
Kikuyu: Kīaga no mūhaka gīgatherio maita maingī na gīkaigwo kīrī kīniaru hingo ciothe.

English: Better health programs involving routine vaccination and strict biosecurity are usually put in place where profit is the main goal
Kikuyu: Mītaratara mīega ya kūrangīra ūgima mwega wa mwīrī hamwe na kūhe njanjo rīrīa kwagīrīire na ūhūthīri wa njīra cia kīmerera cia kūrūa na mīrimū nīirumagīrirwo rīrīa muoroto nī wa kuona ūmithio mūnene.

English: What is intensive production systems?
Kikuyu: Hihi mūtaratara wa kūhingīra ngūkū arī njīra ya ūbacīrīri mūnene wa kūrīithia ngūkū nī ūrīkū?

English: This production system requires high initial capital investment (in breeds, feed, housing, health etc.) than the other two systems
Kikuyu: Mūtaratara ūyū nī urī ngarama nene kūwambīrīa ūkīringithania na mītaratara iyo ingī(Ngarama ici nī hamwe na mūthemba wa ngūkū, kwaga kīaga, kūrangīra ūgima mwega wa mwīrī wa ngūkū na maūndū mangī)

English: Flock sizes range from more than 200 to over 1000 chickens
Kikuyu: Rūūru kaingī rūkoragwo na ngūkū makīria ma magana meerī nginya ngiri īmwe.

English: Main ones are deep litter (sawdust on the floor) and cage system.
Kikuyu: Njīra iria īhūthagīrwo mūno nīkīaga kīa hamwe kīa maranda na tūthandūku twakītwo kīaga thīinī.

English: Used for large scale rearing of broilers and layers Deep
Kikuyu: Nī īhūthīkaga ūrīithi-inī wa ngūkū cia nyama na cia matumbī kwa wūingī.

English: What is Deep litter system?
Kikuyu: Hihi ūrīithi wa kūhingīra ngūkū kiaga-inī gīitītwo matandīko nī ūrīkū?

English: Your chicken are kept indoors all the time, but they have large space to move around freely.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ikoragwo ihingīirwo mahinda me mothe, no nīikoragwo na wīyathi wa kūrūra nīundū wa kīwanja kīariī.

English: The floor is covered with a deep litter of straw, wood shavings, saw dust or other suitable materials.
Kikuyu: floo īkoragwo īhumbīrītwo na mahuti ma nyeki nyūmū, maranda, muura kana indo ingīta ici.

English: Other materials are paddy husk, peanut shells, paddy chaff, chopped straw and such other materials that absorb moisture well can be used depending upon the cost and availability
Kikuyu: Indo ingīnīta makore na mūcere, makoni ma njūgūkaranga, makoni ma mūcere, nyeki nyūmū ndinangie, na indo ingīta icio ingīhota kūnyua maaī noihūthike kūringana na wūingī.

English: What is Free-Range Systems?
Kikuyu: Hihi ūrīithi wa ngūkū kūrekererio ciīmathīre nī ūrīkū?

English: Under this system, chicken are not restricted nor provided with any shelter.
Kikuyu: Ūrīithi wa mūthemba ūyū, ngūkū itigiragīrīrio kana ikahingīrwo nyumba.

English: Birds are left to scavenge for food freely.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū irekagwo ikeyethera irio.

English: The flock contains birds of different types and different ages.
Kikuyu: Rūūru rūkoragwo na ngūkū cia mīhīrīga yothe na riika itiganu.

English: Simple shelters may be provided or the birds share houses with people. Birds can roost outside and nest in the bush.
Kikuyu: Ciaga itarī na ngarama no ciakwo kana igaikara hamwe na andū. Ngūkū noirekerie matumbī gīthaka-inī.

English: What is Backyard Systems?
Kikuyu: Hihi ūrīithi wa kūrekereria ngūkū ciīmathīre irio nī ūrīkū?

English: This is the most common chicken production method for rearing chicken all over Kenya.
Kikuyu: īno nīyo njīra īrīa īhūthikaga mūno ūrīithi-inī wa ngūkū gūkū Kenya?

English: Chicken are housed at night but allowed to roam freely during the day.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ikahingīrwo mathaa ma ūtukū na mūthenya irekwo ciīyethere irio mūthenya wothe.

English: Chicken are fed with small amounts of home-made chicken feed or maize grains before you can release them in the morning.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū iheagwo gīthimi kīnini kīa irio ithondekeirwo mūciī kana mbembe mbere ya ihingūrīirwo.

English: When chickens come back in the evening they are again fed with supplements.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ciacoka hwaī-inī, icokaga igaikīrīo irio nini.

English: Other feed types such as kitchen leftovers can be thrown to them in the course of the day.
Kikuyu: Irio cia mūthemba ūngī ta matigari ma riiko no ciīrio.

English: What are the advantages of deep litter systems?
Kikuyu: Moguni ma ciaga cia gūita mahuti nīmarīkū?

English: It is economical than cage systems.
Kikuyu: Ūrīithi ūyū nīūrī raithi gūkira wa kūhingīra kīnji-inī.

English: Hygienic, comfortable and safe to birds
Kikuyu: Njīra īno nīīrī ūtheru, ngūkū ikaiganīra na igakorwo irī ngitīre kuumana na thūū.

English: Built up litter supplies vitamin B12 and Riboflavin to the birds
Kikuyu: Mahuti ma kīaga maingīha no mahe ngūkū vitamin B12 na riboflavin.

English: Easy to control diseases and vices
Kikuyu: Nī ūhūthū kūgirīrīria mīrimū na mītugo mīuru ta kūrīana.

English: It increases the efficiency of production
Kikuyu: Nī yongagīrīra umithio wa ngūkū.

English: What are some of the tips for managing deep litre system
Kikuyu: Thiri cia gūtungata ngūkū na njīra njega thīinī wa kīaga kīa hamwe kīa gūita mahuti nī irīkū?

English: The deep litter system should always kept dry.
Kikuyu: Kīaga gīite mahuti gīa kūrīithia ngūkū nyingī kiagīrīire gūikara kīrī kīniaru hingo ciothe.

English: Only right numbers of birds should be housed
Kikuyu: Ngūkū itiagīrīire gūkīra namba īrīa yagīrīire kīaga-inī.

English: The house should be well ventilated
Kikuyu: kīaga kīagīrīire gūkorwo na huhīrio njega cia rīera.

English: The litter should be stirred at least once in a week; wet litter if any should be replaced immediately with new dry litter and birds must be fed a balanced ratio.
Kikuyu: Mahuti ma kīaga mabatiī kuurugucanio narī rita rīmwe o kiumia, marīa mahehu macenjio narua na ngūkū īheo irio iria njagīrīru.

English: The time starting deep litter system should be in the dry period of the year as it allows sufficient time (At least two months) for bacterial action.
Kikuyu: Kahinda kega ga kwambīrīria ūrīithi ūyū wa kīaga kīa hamwe kīa mahuti nīrīrīa gūtarī mbura nīgetha bacteria iria njega ikorwo ciethondeka wega.

English: Placing of water should be given due attention to keep litter dry
Kikuyu: Harīa maaī mekuigwo hagīrīire gwīcirio nīgetha kīaga gīikare gīkīniaru.

English: What are the advantages of cage chicken farming?
Kikuyu: Mawega ma kūhingīra ngūkū kīnji-inī thīinī wa kīaga nī marīkū?

English: You can rear more birds per unit of area
Kikuyu: No ūrīithie ngūkū nyingī thīinī wa gīcigo kīnini.

English: Facilitates correct maintenance of records and identifying of poor producers and prompt culling
Kikuyu: Nīgūteithagia kūiga rekodi na njīra hūthū, ūkamenya itarī na umithio na ūkahota gūcieheria.

English: Easier to control poultry cannibalism and egg eating
Kikuyu: Njīra īno īteithagia kūrigīrīria ngūkū kūrīana na kūrīa matumbī.

English: It helps in production of cleaner eggs
Kikuyu: No īteithagia kūngūngania matumbī matheru.

English: Easy control of parasitic disease like coccidiosis and worm infestation
Kikuyu: Nī īteithagi kūrigīrīria gūtamba kwa mīrimū ta kīhuruto na mīnyoo.

English: Egg production, feed efficiency and egg weight of caged layers is more than in deep litter systems according to research
Kikuyu: kūringana na ūthuthuria, mīrekerie ya matumbī, kūnyihia ūitangi wa irio na ūritū wa matumbī ma ngūkū ihingīrītwo kīnjī-inī nī wongererekaga gūkira īrīa cia marīa ma ngūkū cīa kūrekio irī hamwe kīaga-inī kīa mahuti.

English: What is the best method of rearing chicken?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra īrīkū njega ya kūrīithia ngūkū?

English: 5 factors should guide you in choosing the best method. They are size of flock, animal welfare, your reason for farming, available resources and finally, bio-safety.
Kikuyu: Nī harī maūndu matano marīa mangīkūhe mwerekera rīrīa ūgūthura njīra īrīa njega. Namo nīmo; wūingī wa ngūkū, ūtungata ūria wagīrīire, gītūmia gia kūrīithia ngūkū, indo iria mūrīmi enacio na mūthia, kūrigīrīria mīrimū na njīra ya kīmerera.

English: Size of flock; if you are doing large scale chicken production, go for intensive system. If you are in subsistence chicken rearing use backyard or free range farming.
Kikuyu: Ūnene wa rūūru; angīkorūo ūrarīithia ngūkū nyingī, rūmīrīra ūrīithi wa gūcihingīra ciaga-inī. Angīkorūo ūrarīithia ngūkū nīūndū wa kūhingīa mabataro maku ta kūrīa, rūmīrīra ūrīithi wa kūrekereria ikemathīra irio.

English: Animal welfare; slatted systems, deep litter, free range and semi intensive chicken rearing is considered to provide chicken with better health unlike cage system with many inherent welfare problems from restricted movements.
Kikuyu: Ūtungata wa ngūkū; kiugū gīa kūgūithia thumu, kiugū gīa gūciga irīhamwe, gūcirekia ciīmathīre,  mūtukanio wa kūhingīra na kūrekia ciīmathīre nīcio njīra iria iheaga ngūkū ūgima mwega wa mwīrīūkīringithania na tūthandūku twakītwo kīaga harīa ngūkū igīaga thīna wa kuonja nīūndū wa kwaga gūceera.

English: Purpose of production; for commercial production, intensive production systems are better than free range systems that has low yields and profitability
Kikuyu: Itūmi cia kūrīithia ngūkū: cia biacara, kūrīithia ngūkū ihingīirwo nīyo njīra njega īrongoragīrīria baita nīūndū nīikūraga narua.

English: Available investments; when you are a beginner in chicken farming, consider starting small in extensive systems then expand to semi intensive farming. Finally, you can invest in intensive farming upon availability of funds.
Kikuyu: Mbeca cia kūambīrīria; ūkīambīrīria iga ngūkū nini na gūcirererie nja ūcoke wongerere o kahora na ūcenjie njīra ya gūcirīithia nginya wone mbeca cia kūrīithia ihingīirwo.

English: Food safety & Hygiene; some methods such as cage chicken farming and slatted floor systems are the most hygienic. They allow separation of birds, eggs and litter from chicken droppings.
Kikuyu: Wagīrīru wa irio na ūtheru: njīra cia ūrīithi ta kūhingīra kīnji-inī na ciugū irī na floo iragūithia kīoro nīcio cigaga ūtheru. Nī cihotithagia kūgayania ngūkū, matumbī na kīoro.

English: What are feeders and how do you clean feeders for chicken?
Kikuyu: Mīharatī ya irio īthondekagwo atīa na īigagwo īrīmī theru atīa?

English: Are used to give food to the chickens. Should be cleaned regularly to prevent spread of diseases
Kikuyu: Mīharatī ītūmīkaga kūhe ngūkū irio. Nī yagīrīire gūthambagio kaingī nīgetha kūgiria gūtherema kwa mīrimū.

English: Should be cleaned regularly to prevent spread of diseases
Kikuyu: Yagīrīire gūthambio kaingī nīguo ūgirie gūtherema kwa mīrimū.

English: Should be adequate for all chickens to feed at the same time One (1) metre trough or a 35 cm (diameter) tube feeder is big enough for 20 adult chickens to eat from
Kikuyu: Yagīrīire gūkorwo irī mīigananu nīgetha ngūkū ikarīyanīra hamwe. Mūharatī wa mita īmwe kana sentimita 35c nī wūiganīte kūrīithia ngūkū 20 ng'ima.

English: Use feeders that do not waste food and limits contamination (cover with wire mesh)
Kikuyu: Hūthīra mīharatīītaretīkīria wūitangi wa irio na ūracimenyerera kuumana na  gīko.

English: Do not fill feeders to the top to avoid wastage (half-fills are better and then refilled periodically)
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ūgwīkīra irio, ndūkaiyūrīrīrie mūno nīguo itigaitangīke. Gwīkīra nuthu nuthu nīguo wega ūcoke wongagīrīre.

English: Feeders can be made from locally available materials like wood, tin can, metal, plastic) in various shapes (long, round etc) or be bought
Kikuyu: Mīharatī no īthondekwo ūkīhūthīra indo iria ironeka raithi ta mbaū, marebe, cuma na plastics. Na mīhinīre ītiganīte kana īgūrwo kuuma nduka-inī.

English: Should be easy to clean, durable, strong, affordable and easy to fill
Kikuyu: Yagīrīire gūkorwo na raithi gūthambia, ītagūthūka naihenya , mīnyitu, ītarī na goro na īna ūhūthū gwīkīra irio.

English: Feeders should be put in easily accessible areas so that most of the droppings are concentrated in areas where they can be easily removed
Kikuyu: Mīharatī yagīrīire kūigwo handū īrakinyīrīka nīguo ndīkaingīre mai ma ngūkū.

English: What are drinkers and how do you clean drinkers for chicken?
Kikuyu: Mīharatī ya maaī nīkīī na īngīigīka īrī mītheru?

English: Used to give drinking water to the chicken
Kikuyu: īhūthīkaga kūhe ngūkū maaī.

English: Should be cleaned regularly to prevent spread of diseases
Kikuyu: ībatiī gūthambio kaingī nīguo kūrigīrīria gūtamba kwa mīrimū.

English: Drinkers should be cleaned and dried at least twice a day and sprayed with disinfectants once a week after thorough cleaning
Kikuyu: Mīharatī ya maaī ībatiī gūthambio na kūniario maita merī o mūthenya na kūhurwo na dawa lita rīmwe o wiki thutha wa kūmīthambia wega.

English: One metre trough or a 35 cm diameter tube drinker is big enough for 40 adult chickens to drink from
Kikuyu: Mūharatī wa ūrahu wa mita īmwe kana wīna warie wa sentimita 35 nī mūiganu kūhe maaī ngūkū mīrongo īna.

English: Should be big enough for all chickens of the same age to drink from
Kikuyu: Mīharatī ino ībatiī gwakwo ūndū īgwītīkīria ngūkū cia rika riothe kūhota kūnywuīra

English: Can be made from locally available material at home or commercial ones bought (metals or plastics)
Kikuyu: No ithondekwo na indo iria ironeka mūciī-inī kana indo cia kūgūrwo (cuma kana plastiki)

English: Should be durable, strong, stable, easy to clean and fill Troughs, cups, hanging plastic containers or nipple drinkers can be used.
Kikuyu: Mīharatī īno ībatiī gūtūra, īkorwo īrī mīrūmu, mīhūthū gūthambia na kūihūria maaī. Mīthemba nīta ikombe, ikombe cia plastiki ingīcurio,  kama mīnyonyi ya kūnyuīra.

English: What is the importance of record keeping?
Kikuyu: Hihi kūiga rekodi nīgwa bata ūrīkū?

English: All chicken farmers should learn to keep simple records (taken daily or weekly) on performance of their production.
Kikuyu: Arīmi othe a ngūkū no mūhaka merute kūiga rekodi cia kūandīka o mūthenya kana thutha wa wiki ciīgie umithio wa cio

English: This will help in early identification of diseases, poor nutrition and other problems so that interventions can be initiated in good time.
Kikuyu: Kūiga rekodi nīgūteithagia kūmenya kūrīo tene mīrimū īkīingīra, wagi wa irio njega na mathīna mangīnīguo ihonia irehwo kūri o tene.

English: What information should be kept in record?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū mabatiī kwandīkwo rekodi-inī?

English: The following information should be recorded by the farmers
Kikuyu: Maūndū maya nīmo mabatiī kwandīkwo nī mūrīmi thīinī wa rekodi;

English: Feed intake: name of the farmer, age in weeks, light exposure (hours), chicken category based on age (chicks, grower, layer/hen, cock), feed intake (gm/day/head)
Kikuyu: Mūigana wa irio iria irarīo: rītwa ria mūrīmi, ūkūrū wa ngūkū kīwiki, ihinda rīrīa ūtheri ūretīkīrio kūingīra kīaga, ngurubu cia ngūkū kūringana na rika(njui, mīera, ngūkū irarekia, na njogoo), mūigana wa irio iria ikūrīo(grams harī o ngūkū o mūthenya)

English: Chicks and brooding: chicks’ growth data, date of brooding, breed, age in days, number of chicks present, mortality data, average body weight (every 15 days), causes of death etc.
Kikuyu: Njui na ūreri: ūhoro wīgiī ūrīa tūcui tūrakūra, mūthī wa gūkomereria, mūthemba wa ngūkū, mūigana wa tūcui, ikuo, ūritū wa njui(igathimwo o thutha wa thikū ikūmi na ithano), itūmi cia ikuo na mangī maingī.

English: Market: number and chicken type sold (hen, cock, grower, chicken), eggs sold, where and to whom sold, eggs/chicken consumed and to whom, income from sale of eggs, gifts and home consumption of eggs and chickens by your family and friends should also be noted, all expenditures for feed or feed ingredients should be registered carefully, noting quantities, price and date of purchase. If you buy feed from feed sellers, note the name of the seller and the time of purchase.
Kikuyu: Thoko: Mūigana na mūthemba wa ngūkū iria nyendie(ngūkū īrarekia, jogoo, mīera), matumbī marīa mendie, kūria mendetio na rītwa ria mūgūri, matumbī na ngūkū iria irītwo, baida kuuma wendia wa ngūkū, iheo, matumbī na ngūkū iria irītwo nīandū a bamīrī na arata, ngarama ciothe cia irio ibatiī kwandīkwo hamwe na mūigana, thogora, na mūthī ūrīa irio icio ciagūrirwo. Angīkorūo ūgūraga irio kuuma kūrīarīa mendagia irio, andīka rīītwa rīa mwendia na ihinda rīrīa wagūra irio icio.

English: Layers: date of brooding/hatching, date of start of laying egg, breed, number of eggs, laying per day/head, number of eggs broken or out of use, number of hens (present, died, transferred), eggs laid, dead chickens, cause of death and chickens given as gifts
Kikuyu: Ngūkū iria irarekia: mūthī wa kūigīra kana gūcarīkia matūmbī, mūthemba wa ngūkū, mūigana wa matumbī, matumbī marīa o ngūkū īrarekia o mūthenya, mūigna wa matumbī marīa makuīte kana matangīhuthīka, mūigana wa ngūkū iria irīkuo, ikuīte kana ithamītio, matumbī marīa marekie, ngūkū iria nguo, itūmia cia ikuo na ngūkū iria iheyanītwo na iheo.

English: Health record sheets: date of vaccination, name of the vaccinator, type of vaccine, name of the disease, method of vaccination, etc.
Kikuyu: Rekodi ya ūgima wa mwīrī: mūthenya ūrīa njanjo yaheyanirwo, rītwa ria mūheani wa njanjo, mūthemba wa njanjo, rītwa ria mūrimū, njīra ya kūheana njanjo, na maūndū mangī.

English: Treatment data: date of treatment, age of the chicken (week), type of drug used, dose of the drug used, method of drug administration (water, feed), etc
Kikuyu: Rekodi ya ūrigitani:mūthī ūrīa ngūkū yarigitwo, ūkūrū wa ngūkū kīwiki, mūthemba wa dawa urīa wahūthīkire, Mūigana wa dawa īrīa ya hūthīkire, njīra ya kūhe dawa (maaī, irio), na mangī maingī.

English: Suitable location of a chicken house.
Kikuyu: Handū hega ha gwaka kīaga kia ngūkū.

English: Select a location that has access to clean water and is free from predators. The site should also have good drainage to prevent the buildup of water, which can create a breeding ground for diseases.
Kikuyu: Thūra handū harī na ūkinyanīria wa maaī matherū na hatarī na thūū cia ngūkū. Handū hau habatiī gūkorwo na tīīri ūraikūrūkia kīguo wega, nīguo kūrigīrīria mīrimū gūithegeera ho.

English: Why do we need a strong and secure chicken coop?
Kikuyu: Bata wa gīterenge kīrūmu na kīna ūgitīīri nī ūrīkū?

English: A strong, well-ventilated chicken coop is essential for the health and well-being of your chickens. Make sure to include nesting boxes and perches inside the coop.
Kikuyu: Gīterenge kīrūmu na kīretīkīria rīera gūceera nīkīabata harī ūgima mwega wa mwīrī na thayū wa ngūkū. Tigīrīra nīwekīra ithandūkū ria kūrekeria na mītī ya kūūmba.

English: What to consider when selecting a site?
Kikuyu: Maūndū ma kūrora mbere ya gwaka kīaga.
