English: The land should be flat, well drained and not swampy
Kikuyu: Harīa ūraka hagīrīire gūkorwo harī haaraganu, hatararūgama maaī na ti itomboya-inī.

English: Location should have a good flow of fresh air.
Kikuyu: Habatiī kūreka rīera rīceere wega.

English: Land should be accessible but not on the highway.
Kikuyu: Hagīrīire gūkorwo harī raithi gūkinyīrīka no tihakuhī mūno na barabara.

English: The site should be far from other poultry farms, piggery or dairy to avoid noise and diseases.
Kikuyu: Hagīrīire gūkorwo heharaya na ciūmba cia ngūkū ingī, nyūmba  ya ngūrwe na kiūgū kīa ng'ombe nīguo gwīthema na inegene na mīrimū.

English: There should be a reliable source of water.
Kikuyu: Nī hagīrīire gūkorwo na maaī mataraga.

English: Where possible, the land should have some trees and grass cover for shade to cool the poultry houses during hot periods
Kikuyu: Kūngīhotekeka, harīa kīaga gīakītwo hakorwo na mītī ya kīruru nīgetha hakorwo hehu hīndī ya riūa.

English: Why construct a chicken house?
Kikuyu: Twakaga kīaga kīa ngūkū nīkī?

English: To protect chickens from extreme weather: rain, sun, cold and winds
Kikuyu: Nīgetha tūgitīre ngūkū kūmana na rīera rītarī rīega ta mbura, riūa, heho na rūhuho.

English: To protect chickens from predators: dogs, cats, snakes, rats, other pests
Kikuyu: Kūgitīra ngūkū kuumana na thū ta ngui, nyau, nyoka, mbīa na indo ingī.

English: To provide shelter for egg laying and broody hens
Kikuyu: Gūciakīra nyūmba ya kūrekeria matumbī na iria irenda gūkomereria.

English: To minimise disease transmission and theft
Kikuyu: Kūnyihia kūgwatania kwa mīrimū na kūiyuo.

English: What are the dimensions of a poultry house at the household level
Kikuyu: Nī ithimo irīkū ihūthīkaga rīrīa tūgwaka nyūmba ya ngūkū itarī nyingī?

English: A simple poultry backyard, open sided house for 500 layers should be 7m x 12m (84 sq m.).
Kikuyu: Gwaka kīaga kīa ngarama ya thī kīa ngūkū ta 500 kīagīrīire gīako na ithimo cia mita 7×12. kana 84sq m.

English: The walls should be short 1m high made of timber, bricks, iron sheets or mad and wattle.
Kikuyu: Thingo ciagīrīire gwakwo irī nguhī, mita īmwe, gīakwo na mbaū, maturubarī, mabati kana ndoro.

English: The roof should be v-shaped (for easy flow of rainwater) made of iron sheets, plastic sheets with papyrus or grass thatched.
Kikuyu: Mambarita magīrīire kūrathwo na mūhianīre wa v, nīguo maaī magaikūrūka wega, magitwo na mabati, mīnyore kana nyeki.

English: The floor can either be marram rammed and cement screened, or marram mixed with limestone and then rammed.
Kikuyu: Floo no īthondekwo na maramu īkorogetwo kana ītukanītio na limestone na īkindīrwo wega.

English: The door should be double leafed either wooden or metallic
Kikuyu: Mūrango wagīrīire gwakwo ūndū ūkūhingūka miena yerī, wīī wa mbaū kana cuma

English: What are the requirements to observe when constructing a chicken house?
Kikuyu: Nīmaūndū marīkū ma bata gūthingatithia rīrīa ūgwaka nyūmba ya ngūkū?

English: The roof should have a steep slope with overhang to allow rain water to run (rain proof)
Kikuyu: Mambarita magīrīire gwakwo maikūrūkīrīire wega na macomokete kuuma harīa nyūmba īkinyīte nīgetha maaī ma mbura maitīke wega.

English: The house should be at least 1.8-2.0m high to enable a person to stand upright
Kikuyu: Nyūmba yagīrīire gūkorwo na ūraihu wa mita 1.8 nginya 2 nīgetha mūndū akahota kūrūgama wega e thīinī.

English: Should have proper lighting (natural or artificial light)
Kikuyu: Yagīrīire gūkorwo na ūtheri mūiganu.

English: Should protect chickens from direct sunlight
Kikuyu: Yagīrīire kūgitīra ngūkū kuumana na riūa.

English: In a rectangular house, the end walls should face East and West to ensure that only the end walls face the hot afternoon sun
Kikuyu: Nyūmba iria ciakagao miena īmwe īmīraihu gūkīra īrīa īngī, mīena īrīa mīkuhī yagīrīire kūrora ithūīro na irathīro nīguo riūa rīgīthūa rītigacine mwena ūrīa mwariī.

English: Surfaces inside should me made from impervious material for ease of cleaning and disinfection
Kikuyu: Mīena ya nathīiniīyagīrīire nīkūgitwo na kīndū gītaranyua maaīnīgetha akorwo arīraithi gūthambia.

English: Wild bird proof
Kikuyu: Mīgitīre kuumana na nyoni cia gīthaka-inī

English: Have enough space to allow enough room for movement
Kikuyu: Tigia handū hanene hegwītīkīria mīthiīre.

English: Should have a good ventilation to allow fresh air circulation: get rid of excess ammonia and dust. Windows on either side. About 0.6m wire mesh at the back, 1m opening in front (2m in hot areas)
Kikuyu: Handū hau habatiī gūītīkīria rīera gūcera wega, na kweheria rīera rīrīa rīūrū mūthemba wa ammonia na rūkūngū. Ndirica ciīkīrwo thingo-inī iroranīte. Wire mesh ya mita 0.6 īkīrwo mwena wa thutha, nakuo nambere īkirwo na wariī wa mita īmwe kana mita igīrī kūndū kūrīa kūrī na ūrūgarī.

English: Prevent inflowing air from directly flowing on chickens
Kikuyu: Rigīrīria rīera kuuma naja kūingīra kwa ngūkū imwe kwa imwe.

English: Should have solid door with a lock and any sharp object should be removed to avoid injury
Kikuyu: Mūrango wīkīrwo na ūkorwo na kūbūri na weherio kīndū o giothe kīngītheca mūndū kana ngūkū.

English: The floor should be kept dry at all time. Stocking density (28-40 kg/m2)
Kikuyu: Floo ībatiī kūigwo īrīniaru hingo ciothe. Mita īmwe ya floo ītarīrwo kilo 28-40 cia ngūkū.

English: Why do we need to keep the coop clean?
Kikuyu: Twagīrīire kūiga kīaga kīrī gītheru nīkī?

English: Regularly cleaning the coop and disposing of any waste or excess bedding will help prevent the spread of diseases.
Kikuyu: Gūtheria kīaga kaingī na kweheria gīko gīothe hamwe ona matandīko marīa matarabarania nīgūgūtūma mīrimū ītige kūtherema.

English: What are the different types of chicken that you know or those found in your locality?
Kikuyu: Nī mīthemba īrīkū ya ngūkū ūī kana īrīa yonekaga itūra rīanyu?

English: The following chicken breeds are currently kept in Kenya: 1. Local/indigenous chicken 2. Improved local/indigenous chicken 3. Hybrid chicken
Kikuyu: Mīthemba īno ya ngūkū nīīrīithagio būrūri-inī wa Kenya: 1. Ngūkū ya gīcenji 2. Ngūkū ya gīcenji jīciarithanie 3. Ngūkū cia kīrīū (hybrid)

English: What are vaccines?
Kikuyu: Njanjo nīndū?

English: Vaccines are biological products that give protection to animals and humans against germs causing diseases. They are used to prevent, and not to treat or cure diseases
Kikuyu: Njanjo nī ciūmbe irī muoyo igitagīra mahiū na andū kuuma na njamuthi iria irehaga mīrimū. Njanjo iteithagia kūrigīrīria, no tikūrigita mīrimū.

English: Vaccines are different from drugs such as antibiotics which are used to treat diseases
Kikuyu: Njanjo nīirī ūtiganu na dawa ta mūthemba wa antibiotics iria ihūthīragwo kūrigita mīrimū.

English: A vaccine only protects against a specific disease it is given for e.g. Newcastle disease vaccine only protects against Newcastle disease
Kikuyu: Njanjo īgitagīra tu kuumana na mūrimū mūna, kwa mūhiano, Njanjo ya mūrimū wa Newscastle īhotaga kūgītīra kuumana na mūrimū wa newcastle.

English: For vaccines to work well, they must be stored, mixed, dosed, and applied correctly
Kikuyu: Nīguo njanjo īrute wīra wega, no mūhaga īigwo wega, ītukanio na njīra njega, ngūkū īheo gīthimo kīrīa kīagīrīire, na iheo ūrīa kwagīrīire

English: What is the importance of vaccinating chicken?
Kikuyu: Hihi bata wa kūhe ngūkū njanjo nī ūrīkū?

English: To prevent chicken diseases such as Newcastle disease, fowl pox, fowl typhoid and other diseases
Kikuyu: Kūrigīrīria ngūkū kūnyitwo nī mīrimū mīna ta Newcastle, fowl pox, fowl typhoid na mīrimū mīthemba īngī.

English: To prevent the spread of zoonotic diseases (such as salmonellosis) from chickens to humans
Kikuyu: Kūrigīrīria gūtherema kwa mīrimū īrīa ītambanagio kuuma kūrīandū na nyamū ta salmonellosis ūrīa ūtambaga kuuma kūrī ngūkū na thūthiī kūrī andū.

English: To prevent spread of diseases from the hen to chicks through the eggs
Kikuyu: Kūrigīrīria mīrimū gūtamba kuuma kūrī ngūkū gūthiī kūri tūcui kūgerera kūrī matumbī.

English: How to handle and store vaccines?
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra ya kūhuthīra na kūiga njanjo wega nī irīkū?

English: Vaccines should be stored under refrigeration temperatures (4-8°C)
Kikuyu: Njanjo ibatiī kūigwo thīinī wa friji kūndū kwīna heho ya gīthimo kia 4-8°C.

English: DO NOT freeze or store vaccines in a freezer
Kikuyu: Ndūkaige njanjo thīinī wa mbarafu

English: Vaccines should be protected from direct sunlight and heat
Kikuyu: Njanjo no mūhaka īgītīrwo kuonana na rīūa na ūrūgarī.

English: I-2 Newcastle disease vaccine can be temporarily stored at the base of a clay water pot kept in a clean dark place in rural areas
Kikuyu: Njanjo ya mūrimū wa Newcastle no īigwo kwa ihinda thīinī wa njungū ya rīumba ria tīīri icagi-inī.

English: Keep vaccines away from children (children should not play with vaccines).
Kikuyu: Njanjo ciigwo kūraya na ciana(Ciana itigathake na njanjo)

English: How to transport vaccines?
Kikuyu: Hihi njanjo ibatiī gūkuo atīa?

English: Chicken farmers going to buy vaccines from agro-veterinary outlets should transport them using 1. Functional cool boxes with ice packs (for thermostable and non-thermostable vaccines)
Kikuyu: Arīmi a ngūkū magīthiī kūgūra njanjo kuuma nduka cia agrovets no mūhaka macikue thīinī wa mathandūku ma mbarafu(njanjo mīthemba īrīa ītathīnagio kana o na īria īthīnagio nī ūrugarī)

English: A closed steel vacuum flask with ice in it (thermostable and non-thermostable) for short distances.
Kikuyu: Kībūyu kīa thamos gīkīrītwo mbarafu(njanjo mīthemba īrīa ītathīnagio kana o na īria īthīnagio nīūrugarī) kwa ihinda inini.

English: Carrier containers carefully wrapped with damp cloth and protected from direct sunlight (thermostable vaccines)
Kikuyu: Indo ingī cia gūkūīra ciohwo wega na ngītambaya gia kūgītīra ītekūona riūa īmwe kwa īmwe. (njanjo mīthemba īria īthīnagio nī ūrugarī na ūtheri)

English: How to give Newcastle vaccine as eye drop or nasal drop?
Kikuyu: Njanjo ya mūrimū wa Newcastle īheyagwo atīa ta matata na ritho kana maniūrū?

English: To achieve a successful vaccination via eye/nasal drop, you require a hypodermic needle, syringe, or a dropper
Kikuyu: Nīguo kūhota kūhe njanjo na njīra ya gūtatīria ritho kana iniūrū, ugūbatara kūthīra cindano njeke mūno kana dropper.

English: Eye drop vaccination produces stronger protection compared to other delivery routes.
Kikuyu: Njanjo ya gūtatīria ritho īrutaga wīra wega gūkīra īngīciothe cia gūtatīīrīa kūndū kūngū.

English: This is because of the lymphoid tissues located just behind the eyes where the vaccine goes to immediately after vaccination which produces stronger immunity
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū ūhotekega nīūndū mīkiha ya lymphoid īrīa īkoragwo thutha wa maitho nīītethagia gūtara njanjo mwīrī-inī wothe na kūrehe ūgitīīri mūnene.

English: What are the directions for carrying out eye/nasal drop vaccination?
Kikuyu: Mwerekera wa kūhe ngūkū njanjo ya maitho na maniūrū nī ūrīkū?

English: When carrying out eye/nasal drop vaccination the farmer should adhere to the following guidelines: Ensure the vaccine to be given through eye/nasal drop is manufactured and clearly indicated for eye/nasal drop route
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ūkūhe njanjo ya maitho na maniūrū wī mūrīmi wagīrīirwo nīkūrūmīrīra motaaro maya: Tigīrīra atī, njanjo īyo īthondeketwo na īkonanīrīrio wega atī yagīrīire gwīkīrwo maitho kana maniūrū.

English: I-2 ND vaccine should be given by eye drop as this helps chickens develop stronger protection
Kikuyu: Njanjo ya mbere na ya keerī yagīrīire kūheo kūhītūkīra maitho nīūndū nīīteithagia ngūkū kūgīa na ūhoti mūnene wa kūrūa na mīrimū.

English: Vaccines indicated for other routes must never be administered as eye/nasal drop to avoid complications
Kikuyu: Njanjo īrīa īrītwo īheo na njīra īngī ndīkaheo kūhītūkīra maitho kana maniūrū nīgūo kūrigīrīria maūndū morū kūhanīka.

English: Open the vaccine vial and the diluent bottle (remove the aluminium seal and the rubber stopper)
Kikuyu: Hingūra cuba ya njanjo hamwe ya kumīcambicura.

English: When reconstituting the vaccine, the diluent temperature should be between (4-8°C)
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ūraringia njanjo, ūrugarī wagīrīire gūkorwo gatagatī-inī ka 4-8°c

English: After firmly attaching the needle to the syringe, take the cap off the needle
Kikuyu: Thutha wa gūthecereria cindano harī syringe, ruta ngunīko īrīa īkunīkīte cindano.

English: Using the needle fixed to a syringe, withdraw some or all of diluent to be used to dilute the vaccine into the syringe through the needle
Kikuyu: ūkihuthira cindano na syringe īyo, tahia gīcambicūri kīrīa kīrahūthīka kūringia njanjo.

English: Inject into the vaccine vial in the middle of the rubber stopper and mix gently (if vacuum is still intact, it will pull some of the liquid into the vial without any pressure from the vaccinator)
Kikuyu: Onoreria cuba-inī īrīa īna njanjo ūthecete gakunīko ka raba na ūtukanie ūmenyereire.

English: Withdraw the mixed vaccine and transfer into the diluent vial and mix well (by moving from side to side). Do not shake violently to form bubbles
Kikuyu: Onoreria njanjo īyo ningie cuba-inī īrīa yuma na gīcambicūri na ūtukanie wega tarīkūhoria. Ndūgathūce nahinya ūndū mūhūyū ūgwīthondeka.

English: Examine each chicken to make sure it is healthy before you vaccinate it
Kikuyu: Thuthuria ngūkū ciaku ūtigīrīre ti ndwaru mbere ya kūhe njanjo.

English: Use a syringe or a dropper to apply one or two drop(s) in either eye or nostril as directed by the manufacturer
Kikuyu: Hūthīra syringe kana kanyamū ka gūtatīria itata rīmwe kana meerī kūhītūkīra maitho kana maniūrū ta ūrīa mūmīthondeki athīrīte..

English: A vaccine will only be effective if the drop (0.03ml) is placed into the eye or nostril and absorbed. To achieve this, wait for about 10 seconds after administering the drop before releasing the chicken
Kikuyu: Njanjo īkūruta wīra angīkorwo itata ria 0.03ml nīrīaingīra thīinī wa ritho kana iniūrū na rīanyuīka. Nīgetha ūndū ūyū wagīrīre, nīwega gweterera Sekondi 10 mbere ya kūrekereria ngükū.

English: If the drop is not completely absorbed, a new drop should be applied
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo njanjo īyo ndīnanyūīka wega, nīwega gūitīīrīria itata rīngī.

English: To prevent the contents of the vial from getting warm against the hands of the vaccinator, divide the contents of the reconstituted vaccine into two or three empty vials and alternate their use while keeping the others in a cool box with ice packs
Kikuyu: Nīgetha kūrigīrīria njanjo kūgīa ūrugarī yanyitwo na moko, ūbatiī kūmīgaya maita meerī kana matatū na ūikare ūgīcenjanagia ūgīcokagia ithandūkū-inī rīna mbarabu.

English: Vaccination should be done under a shade (of house or tree) away from direct sunlight
Kikuyu: Njanjo yagīrīire kūheyanīrwo kīruru-inī kīa nyūmba kana mūtī īgitīīrītwo kuumana na ūtheri wa riūa.

English: What are the direction for giving Newcastle vaccine in drinking water
Kikuyu: Mwerekera wa kūhe ngūkū njanjo ya Newcastle thīinī wa maaī nī ūrīkū?

English: When administering the Newcastle vaccine through drinking water, consider the following guidelines: Do not open or mix the vaccine until ready to vaccinate
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ūkūhe njanjo ya Newcastle thīinī wa maaī, rūmīrīra mītaratara īno: Ndūkahingūre kana ūtukanie njanjo nginya wīharīrie kūmīheana.

English: Remove all medication, sanitizers and disinfectants from the drinking water 48-72 hours prior to vaccination and up to 24 hours after vaccination (48 for antimicrobials)
Kikuyu: Tigīrīra maaī marīa ūrahūthira matirī na dawa kana indo cia kūūraga njamuthi kwa mathaa 48-72 mbere ya kūhe njanjo na mathaa 24 thutha wa kūhe njanjo, mathaa 48 akorwo nī dawa cia kūūraga bacteria.

English: Provide sufficient drinkers so that all the chickens can drink at one time. Clean and rinse all the waterers thoroughly before use. Withdraw water from the chickens for 1-2 hours prior to vaccination to stimulate thirst (30-60 minutes in hot climates; 60-90 minutes in cool climates)
Kikuyu: Tigīrīra mīharatī ya maaī nī mīiganu nīguo ngūkū ciothe inyue hīndī īmwe. Thambia na ūkamūre mīharatī yothe ya maaī mbere ya kūhūthīra. Ndūkaigīre maaī kwa ihinda rīa ithaa rīmwe kana meerī mbere ya kūhe maaī marī na njanjo nīguo ikorwo inyotiī.

English: Boil the water (NOT tap water) to be used in reconstitution and allow it to cool
Kikuyu: Camūkia maaī(no ti ma mūberethi) marīa mekūhūthīka kūringia ūcoke ūreke mahore.

English: Open the vaccine vial by removing the aluminum seal and rubber stopper
Kikuyu: Hingūra cuba ya njanjo na njīra ya kūruta ngūnīko ya aluminium na raba.

English: Using the water that will be used in reconstitution, fill vaccine vial two-thirds with clean, cool water and mix gently in order to reconstitute the vaccine
Kikuyu: Ringia njanjo ūkīhūthīra maaī marīa ūharīirie, matheru na mahoru, na njīra ya kūmekīra thīinī wa cuba ya njanjo nginya ūkinyie gīcunjī-inī kia igīrī harī ithatū gia cuba iyo ūcoke ūthuke kahora kahora.

English: Rinse the vaccine vial three times to remove all the vaccine
Kikuyu: Kamūra cuba iyo ya njanjo maita matatū nīguo ūrute njanjo yothe.

English: Estimate the average daily water consumption then calculate 30% of the volume of water to prepare the vaccine
Kikuyu: Thuthuria mūigana wa maaī marīa mahūthagīrūo o mūthenya na thutha ūcio ūhūthīre 30% ya mūigana wa maaī macio kūharīria njanjo.

English: Addition of skim milk powder (at the rate of 500g/200L) to the water 20-30 minutes before adding the vaccine is recommended to stabilize the water
Kikuyu: Kūongererwo kwa iria ria mūtu (kwa wūigana wa 500g/200L) maaī-inī macio ndagīka 20 nginya 30 mbere ya kūongerera njanjo icio nī kwagīrīire nīgetha īrute wīra wega.

English: Mix with stock solution after adding the vaccine stabilizer following manufacturer’s instructions
Kikuyu: Thuka mūtukanio ūyū wega thutha wa kūongerera stabilizer ya njanjo īyo kūringana na mūmīthondeki.

English: Distribute the vaccine solution to the waterers placed where there is no direct sunlight
Kikuyu: Gayanīria njanjo iyo ningie mīharate ya maaī iria īigītwo kūndū ītara kinyīrwo nī ūtheri wa riūa.

English: Do not provide any other drinking water until all of the vaccine mixture has been consumed
Kikuyu: Ndūkaigīre ngūkū maaī mangī nginya rīrīa irīnina macio mongereirwo njanjo.

English: Check to ensure all the birds are drinking water, if not, redistribute the drinkers as necessary
Kikuyu: Thuthuria wone ngūkū ciothe nīiranyua maaī, na angīkorwo imwe itiranyua, thongithia mīharatī īyo ya maaī ūndū īgūkinyīrwo nī ngūkū ciothe.

English: The chickens should drink all the vaccine solution within 1-2 hours (never in less than 1). When the vaccine is consumed too fast, other chickens will not have an opportunity to drink
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ibatiī kūnina maaī maya mongereirwo njanjo ithaa-inī rīmwe nginya meerī(itingīnina ithaa rīmwe rītarī ithiru). Njanjo ino īngīnyuo naihenya mūno, ngūkū imwe nociage mweke wa kūmīnywa.

English: Record all vaccine information as well as any challenges/problems which may occur with the chickens or vaccination process
Kikuyu: Niwega kūiga rekodi cia njanjo iyo na mathīna marīa ingīrehe kūrī ngūkū kana ona hīndīya kūhe

English: What are the common causes of Newcastle vaccine failure?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī maūndū marīkū kaingī mangītuma njanjo ya mūrimū wa Newcastle ītoorio?

English: The Newcastle vaccine may fail because of the following: Giving Newcastle vaccines incorrectly (wrong route, poor reconstitution, wrong dosage)
Kikuyu: Njanjo ya Newscastle no ītoorio nīūndū wa maūndū maya: Kūhūthīra njanjo ya mūrimū wa Newcastle na njīra ītagīrīire (kūhe na njīra ītagīrīire, kūringia na njīra njūru, mūigana ūrīa ūtagīrīire)

English: Poor handling of live Newcastle vaccines (breakdown in cold chain)
Kikuyu: Kūiga njanjo ya mūrimū wa Newcastle na njīra njūrū(gūthūka īrī thitoo-inī ya mbarafu)

English: Using tap water/chlorinated water to reconstitute Newcastle vaccines
Kikuyu: Kūhūthīra maaī ma mūberethi kana maaī marī dawa ya chroline kūringia njanjo ya mūrimū wa Newcastle.

English: In some cases, the field strain of an organism is very strong, and the vaccine strain is weak.
Kikuyu: Rīmwe na rīmwe, mūrimū no ūkorwo ūrīna na hinya mūingī kūrī njanjo ya kūūrigīrīria.

English: In this situation, the flock may be effectively vaccinated, but the immunity is insufficient to protect against the disease completely
Kikuyu: Ūndū-inī ta ūyū, rūūru rwothe no rūheo njanjo, na rīngī thutha-inī rūage hinya mūiganu wa kūīgitīra kuuma na mūrimū.

English: Failure to observe hygiene and sanitary conditions allows infectious disease agents build up.
Kikuyu: kwaga kūgacerithia ūtheru nī gūtumaga mīrimū mīūrū ya kūgwatanio īthereme.

English: it is possible that the challenge dose of a particular infectious agent will be so great, or so soon, that a normally effective vaccination program will be overwhelmed
Kikuyu: Nī kūrī ūhoteku atī njanjo ya gūcokerithia no īkorwo īrī na ūguni mūnene harī kūrūa na mūrimū , kana o naihenya mūno, mūtaratara mweiga wa njanjo ūgūmanīrio nī mūrimū.

English: The immune status of the parent flock also can contribute to a vaccine failure.
Kikuyu: Hinya wa rūrū rwa gūciarithania kūrūa na mīrimū no kūhotomie hinya wa njanjo kūruta wīra wega kūrī njiarwa.

English: If the breeder flock provides progeny with high levels of maternal antibodies, vaccination during the first 2 weeks of life may result in the vaccine being neutralized
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo rūūru rwa gūciarithania rūciaraga njīarwa īrī na hinya wa kūrūa na mīrimū, kūhe njanjo wiki-inī igīrī cia gūciarwo gūtingīteithia.

English: Certain infectious diseases that lower immunity may result in vaccine failure
Kikuyu: Mīrimū īmwe mīūrū ya gūtambanio īrīa īnyihagia hinya wa kūrūa na mīrimū nīīhotomagia hinya wa njanjo kūruta wīra wega.

English: Once reconstituted, vaccines should be used within 30-45 minutes, any that remain should be discarded.
Kikuyu: Njanjo yarīkia kūringio, no mūhaka īhūthīrwo ndagīka-inī mīrongo ītatū nginya mīrongo īna na ītano, nayo īrīa īgūtigara īteo.

English: What are the benefits of rearing Kienyeji chicken
Kikuyu: Hihi ūgūni wa kūrīithia ngūkū cia gīcenji nī ūrīkū?

English: Superior Meat and Eggs: Renowned for their flavorful meat and rich-yolked eggs, indigenous breeds command premium prices compared to commercial chickens.
Kikuyu: Nyama na matumbī mega: Ngūkū icio nī ciūīkaine mūno nīūndū wa nyama īrī cama na matumbī marī mūruru mūnene, ningī ngūkū ici nīirī goro ūkīringithania na mithemba ingī īrīa īreragwo irī ya biacara.

English: Low Investment: Compared to their commercial counterparts, kienyeji chickens require less initial investment in housing and feed, making them accessible to small-scale farmers.
Kikuyu: Mbeca nini cia kūambīrīra: Ūrīithi wa ngūkū cia gīcenji ūbataraga mbeca nini gūciakīra ona kūgūrīra irio hīndī ya kūambīrīria ūkīringithania na iria ireragwo irī cia biacara na nīūndū ūcio arīmi anini makahota gūcirīithia.

English: Hardy and Disease-Resistant: Adapted to local conditions, kienyeji chickens flourish on modest feed and are naturally resistant to many common poultry diseases.
Kikuyu: Nīīkoragwo na ūhoti wa kūhiūrania na mīrimū: Ngūkū cia kienyeji nīciīhaaragīria mīikarīre ya kūrīa ciikaraga, na nīciīhotaga kūhiūrania na mīrimū mīingīīrīa īnyitaga ngūkū.

English: Sustainable and Eco-Friendly: Their foraging nature reduces reliance on purchased feed, minimizes environmental impact, and enriches soil with their droppings.
Kikuyu: Nīitūūraga na itithūkagia marīa macirigicīirie mūno: Nīūndū wa gūīcarīria irio, itibataraga kūgūrīrwo irio hingo ciothe, ūndū ūyū ūkanyihia wanangi wa marīa matūrigicīirie na igateithia kūno ria tīīri na njīra ya kūrekia kīoro gīacio.

English: How do you source chickens?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingīruta ngūkū kū?

English: Hatching your own: Requires a rooster and brooder setup but offers superior control over genetics and potential profit from selling chicks.
Kikuyu: Gūcarīkīthia njui ciaku: Ūndū ūyū ūrabatara njogo na gūthondeke handū hakūrerera tūcui no nīūhotithagia wamūri mūnene wa mūthemba wa ngūkū iria igūcarīkio na baida īrīa īkuoneka wendia-inī wa njui.

English: Buying Day-old Chicks: Hatcheries like KENCHIC and KALRO Naivasha offer vaccinated chicks of improved indigenous breeds, reducing management complexities.
Kikuyu: Kūgūra tūcui twa ūkūrū wa mūthenya ūmwe: Mīgūnda ya gūcarīkithiaa tūcui ta KENCHIC na KALRO Naivasha nī yendagia tūcui twa gīcenji tūhetwo njanjo, ūndū ūrīa ūhūthagia mītungatīre.

English: Characteristics of a Local indigenous chicken?
Kikuyu: Hihi maūthī ma ngūkū cia gīcenji nī marīkū?

English: This is the most common type of chicken kept by many smallholder farmers
Kikuyu: ūyū nī mūthemba wa ngūkū ūrīa ūreragwo kaingī nī arīmi aingī arīa anini.

English: They are well adapted to local conditions, look for their own food, and are more resistance to diseases.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ici nīimenyerete kūrīa ireragīrwo, nī cīīyethagīra irio, na nī ciītiragia mīrimū.

English: They vary a lot in size, colours and appearance.
Kikuyu: Ikoragwo irīna ngūrani nene ya ūrīa cigana mwīrī, marangi ona ūrīa ihanaga.

English:
They grow more slowly, lay less eggs (about 60 eggs in a year) and do not add a lot of weight
compared to commercial breeds even when they fed on commercial feeds.
Kikuyu: Ikūraga kahora, ikarekia matumbī manini(ta mīrongo ītandatū o mwaka) na itiīkīraga ūritū mūnene ta mīthemba irīa īreragwo īrī ya biacara ona ingīheo irio njega cia kūgūra.

English: Characteristics of a Improved local/indigenous chicken
Kikuyu: Maūthī ma ngūkū njiarithanie cia gīcenji

English: These are chicken that have been selected from among local chicken based on their ability to grow faster or lay more eggs than others.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ici nīiria ithurītwo kuuma ngūkū-inī cia gīcenji kūringana na ūhoti wacio wa gūkūra na ihenya kana kūrekia matumbī maingī gūkīra iria ingī.

English: The chickens are then reared in a way that makes these characteristics even better.
Kikuyu: Thutha ūcio ngūkū ici nīireragwo na njīra ya kwagīrithia maūthī maya.

English: The improved local chicken varies in size, colour and appearance.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū cia gīcenji njīarithanie nīirīūtiganu wa kīmwīrī, marangi ona ūrīa ihana.

English:
They can only be distinguished from the unimproved local indigenous chicken from their growth characteristics and ability to lay eggs. Examples are KALRO Kienyeji,
Rainbow Rooster and Kuroiler
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ici ciamūranagio na iria itarī njiarithie kūringana na ūhoti wacio wa gūkūra naihenya na kūrekia matumbī maingī. Ngerekano nīKALRO Kienyeji, Rainbow Rooster na Kuroiler.

English: Characteristics of a Hybrid/commercial chicken
Kikuyu: Maūthī ma ngūkū cia biacara kana iria njiarithanīe irongoreirio biacara

English: These are chicken that were introduced in Kenya from European countries and are not well adapted to local conditions.
Kikuyu: Ici nī ngūkū iria ciokire Kenya kuuma mabūrūri-inī ma rūraya na itiamenyerete wega rīera ria gūkū.

English: They are kept in chicken houses and mostly feed on commercial feeds.
Kikuyu: Ngūkū ici īhingagīrwo ciaga-inī na kaingī irīaga irio cia kūgūrwo.

English: They are two types – broilers and layers which are kept for meat and eggs respectively.
Kikuyu: Ikoragwo irī mīthemba īrī- cia nyama kana broilers na cia matumbī kana layers

English: They require more care than local chicken and are susceptible to diseases
Kikuyu: Nī biataraga kūrūmbūiyo makīria gūkīra ngūkū cia kūu na nīihotagwo nī mīrimū.

English: What are the benefits of keeping chickens?
Kikuyu: Hihi maūguni ma kūrera ngūkū nī marīkū?

English: Keeping chickens is one of the easiest ways to live more sustainably.
Kikuyu: Kūrīithia ngūkū nī njīra īmwe hūthū ya gūtūūra mūtūūrīre wa mwega.

English: They help eliminate scraps, produce a natural fertiliser, get rid of pesky garden pests, assist with composting and provide fresh, free-range eggs!
Kikuyu: Nīiteithagia kweheria indo iria ciatigio, gūthondeka fertilizer ya ndūire, kweheria tūnyamū tūrīa tūthūkagia mīgūnda, gūteithīrīria wīra wa gūbūthia indo, na gūtuhe matumbī mega.

English: Keeping chickens is one of the most rewarding ways to start living green.
Kikuyu: Kūrīithia ngūkū nī njīra īmwe njega mūno ya kwambīrīria mūtūūrīre mwega ūtarīwa gūthūkia marīa matūrigicīirie

English: How to breed and manage cocks?
Kikuyu: Hihi njamba iciarithagio na gūtungatwo atīa?

English: One cock should be used to serve 10 hens
Kikuyu: Njamba īmwe yagīrīire kūheyo mīera ikūmi.

English: Cock replaced after 2 years or less to avoid inbreeding
Kikuyu: Njamba ībatiī gūcenjio thutha wa mīaka īrī nīgetha gwīthema ūciari wa nyina na nyina

English: Improved indigenous chickens can be used to improve indigenous chickens (kienyeji)
Kikuyu: Ngūkū cia gīcenji iria njiarithanie no ihūthīke gūthondeka ngūkū cia gīcenji itarī njiarithanie

English: Layers should be kept for about 2 years
Kikuyu: Ngūkū cia kūrekia ibatiī kūigwo ihinda ria mīaka īrī.

English: Hens can lay eggs for around 4-5 years before running out (chick-menopause state)
Kikuyu: Ngūkū no irekie ihinda rīa mīaka īna nginya ītano igacoka ikaingīra kahinda karīa itarekagia

English: Hybrid layers genetically bred for eggs usually run out of eggs earlier/faster (2 years)
Kikuyu: Ngūkū iciarithanītio irī cia biacara itigaga kūrekia tene thutha wa ihinda ria miaka īrī

English: What to consider when constructing a Chicken house?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī maūndū marīkū magīrīirwo gwīcirīrio hīndī ya waki wa kiaga kia ngūkū?

English: Consider factors like ventilation, predator proofing, and space (2 sq ft per bird) when designing your poultry house.
Kikuyu: Wīcirie ūhoro wa maūndū ta rīera rīega thīinī wa kīaga, ūgitīīri kuuma kūrī thūū, na handū harī na warīe mūiganu(futi imwe kwa imwe harī o ngūkū) rīrīa ūrathondeka kīaga kia ngūkū.

English: Locally available materials like timber and mud walls can be cost-effective.
Kikuyu: Indo iria cionekaga kūrīa mūciī ta mbaū na rīūmba no ihūthīke ītanjīra ya kūnyihia ngarama ya waki.
